"Element 79 in the periodic table is a dense, soft, lustrous yellow metal that is highly malleable and ductile. It occurs in nature chiefly as the free metal in alluvial and lode deposits, and as a component of certain telluride ores. Its chemical symbol is Au, derived from the Latin word 'aurum.' It has a melting point of 1,064.18 °C and a boiling point of 2,856 °C. It is the most malleable and ductile of all metals: a single gram can be beaten into a sheet of about 1 square meter, or drawn into a wire over 2 kilometers long. It is chemically resistant to most acids but dissolves in aqua regia (a mixture of concentrated nitric and hydrochloric acids) and in cyanide solutions in the presence of oxygen."

Una caja contiene 48 galletas que se reparten en partes iguales entre 6 niños. ¿Cuántas galletas recibe cada niño?
A train carriage has 80 seats. If 53 seats are occupied, how many seats are empty? Output only the final number.
What is 25% of 80?
An athlete runs 4 miles every day. How many miles does the athlete run in total over a period of 7 days? Output only the final number.
A jar contains 135 marbles. If 47 marbles are removed, how many marbles remain?
A baker packs 36 cookies into boxes, putting 4 cookies in each box. How many boxes of cookies are packed in total? Output only the final number.
What is the perimeter of a square whose side length is 9 meters?
If a standard deck of 52 playing cards has 12 face cards, how many non-face cards are in the deck? Output only the final number.
A book has 14 chapters. Each chapter has 12 pages. How many pages are in the book?
Uma livraria tem 50 livros didáticos. Se eles vendem 15 desses livros, quantos livros didáticos restam na livraria? Forneça apenas o número final.
Un agricultor tiene 5 filas de manzanos. Cada fila tiene 9 manzanos. ¿Cuántos manzanos tiene en total?

A company starts with 150 employees. In the first year, they lay off 20% of their staff. In the second year, they hire 45 new employees. How many employees does the company have at the end of the second year?

A rectangular garden has a length that is 3 meters longer than twice its width. The owner installs a diagonal walkway from one corner to the opposite corner, and the walkway measures exactly 15 meters. Find the area of the garden in square meters.
A farmer harvests 400 apples. He sells 35% of them at the local market and then packages the remaining apples into boxes of 10. How many boxes of apples does the farmer package?
A store sells notebooks for $4 each and pens for $2 each. Maria buys 3 notebooks and twice as many pens as notebooks. She pays with a $20 bill. How much change, in dollars, does she receive?

A water tank contains 250 liters of water. Water leaks out at a rate of 3 liters per hour. If 50 liters of water are added to the tank after 12 hours of leaking, how many liters of water are in the tank at that moment?
A car travels 60 km at a constant speed of 40 km/h, then another 60 km at a constant speed of 60 km/h. What is the average speed, in km/h, for the entire 120 km trip?

A store owner buys a jacket for $80. She increases the price by 50% for retail sale. During a clearance sale, she offers a 20% discount on the retail price. What is the final sale price of the jacket in dollars?

A tank contains 240 liters of water. Two pipes fill it: Pipe A adds 15 L/min and Pipe B adds 25 L/min. A drain removes 10 L/min. If all three operate simultaneously, how many minutes will it take to fill the tank from 240 L to its 600 L capacity?

Um estudante comprou um livro por R$ 50. Ele vendeu o livro com um lucro de 40% sobre o preço de compra. Mais tarde, ele comprou o mesmo livro de volta com um desconto de 20% sobre o preço pelo qual o havia vendido. Quanto o estudante pagou para comprar o livro de volta, em reais?
Un comerciante compra 15 cajas de naranjas. Cada caja contiene 24 naranjas y cada naranja pesa en promedio 200 gramos. ¿Cuántos kilogramos pesan en total todas las naranjas?
A train travels at a constant speed of 90 km/h for 2 hours, and then increases its speed to 120 km/h for the next 3 hours. What is the total distance traveled by the train in kilometers?
A recipe for 8 servings requires 3 cups of flour and 2 cups of sugar. How many cups of flour are needed to scale the recipe to 20 servings, assuming the sugar is also scaled proportionally?
A baker makes 120 cookies. She sells 60% of them in the morning. In the afternoon, she sells 50% of the remaining cookies. How many cookies does she have left at the end of the day?

A rectangular garden has a length that is 3 meters longer than twice its width. The owner installs a diagonal walking path that is exactly 15 meters long. What is the area of the garden, in square meters?
A rectangular garden has a length of 15 meters and a width of 8 meters. If the length is increased by 20% and the width is decreased by 25%, what is the new area of the garden in square meters?
An investor puts $5,000 into an account that earns 4% simple annual interest. No deposits or withdrawals are made. How much total interest, in dollars, will have been earned after 6 years?
A container holds 8 liters of a 15% saline solution. If 4 liters of pure water are added to the container, what is the concentration of the new saline solution as a percentage?
A school orders 14 dozen donuts for a staff meeting. If 23 donuts are left over at the end, how many donuts were eaten?

Uma caixa d'água tem capacidade de 1000 litros e está com 400 litros. Uma torneira é aberta e despeja 15 litros de água por minuto na caixa, enquanto um ralo escoa 5 litros de água por minuto. Depois de quantos minutos a caixa d'água estará completamente cheia?
Una clase tiene 40 estudiantes. El 35% practica fútbol, el 25% practica baloncesto y el resto no practica ningún deporte. ¿Cuántos estudiantes no practican ningún deporte?

A bakery sells cookies in boxes of 6 for $5.00, or individually for $1.00 each. If a customer buys exactly 20 cookies using a combination of boxes and individual cookies, what is the minimum total cost in dollars?

A rectangular garden has a length of 18 meters and a width of 7 meters. A path of uniform width 1 meter is built around the outside of the garden, with the path's outer edge forming a larger rectangle. What is the area, in square meters, of the path alone?

A factory operates two machines, A and B. Machine A produces 150 widgets per hour and runs for 6 hours per day. Machine B produces 120 widgets per hour and runs for 8 hours per day. What is the total number of widgets produced by both machines combined over a 5-day period?
A car travels at a constant speed of 65 km/h for 2 hours 40 minutes. How many kilometers does it travel?

A container initially holds 40 liters of a 20% salt solution. If 10 liters of pure water are added to the container, what is the concentration of salt in the new solution, expressed as a percentage? Provide only the numerical value.
A store sells notebooks for $4 each and pens for $2 each. Maria buys 3 notebooks and twice as many pens as notebooks. She pays with a $50 bill. How much change does she receive?
An arithmetic progression has a first term of 7 and a common difference of 4. What is the sum of the first 12 terms of this progression?
A water tank contains 240 liters. Water is drained at a rate of 15 liters per minute for 8 minutes, then refilled at 20 liters per minute for 6 minutes. How many liters are in the tank at the end?
Em uma fazenda, há galinhas e coelhos. O número total de cabeças é 35 e o número total de patas é 94. Quantos coelhos há na fazenda?
Un depósito contiene 500 litros de agua. Se extraen 3/5 del contenido y luego se añaden 80 litros más. ¿Cuántos litros quedan en el depósito?
A car travels at a speed of 60 mph for 2 hours and then at a speed of 75 mph for 3 hours. What is the average speed of the car, in miles per hour, over the entire 5-hour journey?
A triangle has sides of length 9 cm, 12 cm, and 15 cm. What is the area of the triangle in square centimeters?

A group of 8 friends went out for dinner. The total bill, which already includes a 15% tip, came to $184. If they split the total bill equally among themselves, how much does each person pay in dollars?
A recipe for 4 servings requires 240 g of flour. How many grams of flour are needed to make 9 servings, keeping the same proportions?
If 3x + 7 = 22, what is the value of 5x - 4?
A sum of $1200 is invested at a simple annual interest rate of 5% for 3 years. What is the total amount in the account at the end of the 3 years?
A rectangular garden has a perimeter of 48 meters. If the length of the garden is 4 meters longer than its width, what is the area of the garden in square meters?
A box contains 5 red, 3 blue, and 2 green marbles. Two marbles are drawn at random without replacement. What is the probability, expressed as a fraction in lowest terms, that both marbles are red?
Uma loja comprou um produto por R$ 80,00 e o revendeu com um lucro de 40% sobre o custo. Qual foi o preço de venda desse produto em reais?
Una clase tiene 24 estudiantes. El 3/8 de los estudiantes son mujeres y el resto son hombres. ¿Cuántos hombres hay en la clase?

A company starts with 150 employees. In the first year, the number of employees increases by 20%. In the second year, the company lays off 15% of its workforce. How many employees does the company have at the end of the second year?

A rectangular garden has a length of 18 meters and a width of 7 meters. A path of uniform width 1 meter is built around the outside of the garden. What is the area, in square meters, of the path alone?
A rectangular garden has a length that is 4 meters longer than its width. If the perimeter of the garden is 56 meters, what is the area of the garden in square meters?
A store sells notebooks for $4 each and pens for $2 each. Maria buys 3 notebooks and 6 pens. She pays with a $30 bill. How much change, in dollars, does she receive?

A store offers a discount on a jacket. The original price of the jacket is $120. It is first discounted by 25%. A customer then uses a coupon that takes an additional 10% off the discounted price. What is the final price of the jacket in dollars?
A train travels at a constant speed of 75 km/h. How many kilometers does it travel in 2 hours and 24 minutes?
In a box of 80 marbles, 35% are red, 45% are blue, and the rest are green. If 5 green marbles are removed from the box, how many green marbles are left?
A cylinder has a radius of 5 cm and a height of 12 cm. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is the volume of the cylinder in cubic centimeters?

Uma loja compra um produto por R$ 80 e o vende com um lucro de 50% sobre o preço de custo. Durante uma liquidação, a loja oferece um desconto de 15% sobre esse preço de venda. Qual é o preço de venda final do produto em reais?

Un depósito contiene 240 litros de agua. Se vacía a razón de 15 litros por minuto durante 10 minutos y luego se llena añadiendo 8 litros por minuto durante 5 minutos. ¿Cuántos litros de agua hay al final en el depósito?

A train travels at a constant speed of 75 miles per hour for 2.4 hours. It then increases its speed to 90 miles per hour and travels for another 40 minutes. What is the total distance traveled by the train in miles?
A school library has 1,200 books. 35% are fiction, 25% are science, and the rest are history. How many history books are in the library?

A bag contains 3 red marbles and 2 blue marbles. Two marbles are drawn at random one after another without replacement. What is the probability that both marbles drawn are red? State your answer as a decimal.
A recipe for 8 servings requires 3 cups of flour. How many cups of flour are needed to make 14 servings, keeping the same proportions?

A farmer harvests 450 kilograms of apples. He sells 60% of the apples at a local market. Of the remaining apples, 20% are used to make cider and the rest are stored. How many kilograms of apples are stored?
A car uses 8 liters of fuel to travel 100 km. How many liters of fuel will it use to travel 325 km?
An investment of $2,000 earns interest at a rate of 5% per annum, compounded annually. What is the value of the investment, in dollars, at the end of 2 years?

A rectangular garden has a length that is 3 meters longer than twice its width. A path of uniform width 1 meter runs around the outside of the garden, increasing the total outer perimeter to 54 meters. What is the area of the garden itself, in square meters?

Três amigos dividem igualmente a conta de um jantar que custa originalmente R$ 240. Antes de dividir, eles aplicam um cupom de desconto de 15% sobre o valor total da conta. Quanto cada amigo pagará em reais após o desconto?
Una tienda ofrece un descuento del 20% sobre un artículo que cuesta $80. Después del descuento, se aplica un impuesto del 10% sobre el precio con descuento. ¿Cuál es el precio final en dólares?

A company distributes bonuses based on three performance tiers. Tier A employees receive a base bonus of $5,000, Tier B employees receive $3,000, and Tier C employees receive $1,500. For each year of tenure beyond 2 years, employees receive a 5% simple interest increase on their base bonus (calculated as a percentage of the base bonus, not compounded). Additionally, employees with a 'Star' rating receive an extra flat $800, but this rating is only eligible for Tier A and Tier B employees who have at least 4 years of tenure. Employees with less than 1 year of tenure receive no bonus regardless of tier.

Consider a team of 4 employees with the following profiles:
- Alice: Tier A, 5 years of tenure, Star rating.
- Bob: Tier B, 3 years of tenure, Star rating.
- Charlie: Tier C, 1 year of tenure, no Star rating.
- David: Tier A, 0.5 years of tenure, Star rating.

What is the total sum of bonuses distributed to this team in dollars? Output only the final numerical value.

A cryptographer designs a 16-character license key using only uppercase letters and digits. The key must satisfy all of the following constraints:

1. Exactly 4 characters are digits and the remaining 12 are letters.
2. No two digits are adjacent.
3. The first and last characters are letters.
4. The digits, read left to right, form a strictly increasing sequence when interpreted as base-10 digits.
5. The letters are chosen from the 26-letter English alphabet and are not required to be distinct.

How many distinct license keys satisfy all five constraints? Give your answer as an integer.

A specialized transport service charges a flat reservation fee of $25 per trip. The distance fare is $2.50 per mile for the first 10 miles, $2.00 per mile for the next 15 miles, and $1.50 per mile for any distance beyond 25 miles. However, there are two special adjustments:
1. If the trip occurs during peak hours (between 4:00 PM and 7:00 PM), a 20% surcharge is applied to the distance fare only (not to the flat fee).
2. If the total distance exceeds 30 miles, the customer receives a flat discount of $10 off the total bill (applied after all surcharges).

A customer takes a trip of 35 miles that starts at 5:30 PM. What is the final cost of the trip in dollars? Output only the final numerical value.
A circle has equation x^2 + y^2 - 6x + 2y - 6 = 0. Find the y-coordinate of the center of this circle.

A factory operates three assembly lines: X, Y, and Z.
- Line X produces 120 widgets per hour, but has a defect rate of 5%.
- Line Y produces 150 widgets per hour, with a defect rate of 4%.
- Line Z produces 200 widgets per hour, with a defect rate of 3%.

During an 8-hour shift, Line X operates for the full 8 hours. Line Y operates for 6 hours. Line Z operates for 5 hours. At the end of the shift, all defective widgets are discarded, and the remaining non-defective widgets are packaged. Each package contains exactly 12 widgets. If there are leftover non-defective widgets that cannot form a complete package of 12, they are stored for the next shift.

How many complete packages of widgets are produced during this shift? Output only the final integer value.
Compute the determinant of the 3x3 matrix [[2,1,3],[0,1,4],[5,6,0]].

A committee consists of 5 seniors, 4 juniors, and 3 sophomores. A subcommittee of 4 members is to be randomly selected. What is the number of possible subcommittees that contain at least 2 seniors and at least 1 junior? Output only the final integer value.
Find the smallest positive integer n such that n is divisible by 2, 3, 4, 5, and 6 simultaneously.

Um fazendeiro deseja construir um cercado retangular para seus animais. Ele tem um rolo de cerca de 120 metros de comprimento. Ele planeja usar uma parede de pedra já existente como um dos lados do cercado, de modo que ele só precise cercar os outros três lados. No entanto, por razões regulatórias, o lado paralelo à parede de pedra deve ter pelo menos 30 metros e não pode exceder 70 metros. Qual é a área máxima possível para o cercado, em metros quadrados, sob essas restrições? Forneça apenas o valor numérico final.
Sea f(x) = 2x^2 - 8x + 3. Encuentra el valor mínimo de f(x) para x real.

A grid of lights has 4 rows and 4 columns. Initially, all 16 lights are turned off. Pressing a button on any cell toggles the state of that cell and all of its horizontally and vertically adjacent neighbors. A programmer presses the buttons at the following coordinates (row, column), indexed from 1 to 4:
- (1, 2)
- (2, 3)
- (3, 1)
- (4, 4)
- (2, 3) (pressed a second time)

How many lights are turned ON at the end of these operations? Output only the final integer value.
In a right triangle, the legs have lengths 5 and 12. Compute the length of the hypotenuse.
What is the remainder when $3^{1000}$ is divided by 13? Output only the final numerical value.
Compute the sum of the first 20 terms of the arithmetic sequence whose first term is 3 and common difference is 7.

A triangle has vertices at A(1, 2), B(5, 5), and C(5, -1) in the Cartesian plane. A point P(x, y) is chosen such that it is the reflection of A across the line passing through B and C. What is the x-coordinate of P? Output only the final numerical value.
Evaluate the limit: lim_{x -> 0} (sin(3x))/(5x).

A water tank can be filled by three pipes: A, B, and C.
- When pipes A and B work together, they can fill the tank in 6 hours.
- When pipes B and C work together, they can fill the tank in 10 hours.
- When pipes A and C work together, they can fill the tank in 7.5 hours.

How many hours will it take for all three pipes working together to fill the tank? Output only the final numerical value.
A bag contains 5 red balls and 3 blue balls. Two balls are drawn at random without replacement. What is the probability that both balls are red? Express your answer as a fraction in lowest terms.

Uma progressão aritmética (PA) de números inteiros tem 20 termos. A soma dos primeiros 5 termos dessa PA é igual a 50, e a soma dos últimos 5 termos é igual a 350. Qual é a razão dessa progressão aritmética? Forneça apenas o valor numérico final.
Calcula el valor de la suma 1 + 2 + 3 + ... + 100.

A company has three departments: A, B, and C. Department A has 40 employees with a mean salary of $50,000. Department B has 60 employees with a mean salary of $60,000. Department C has 100 employees with a mean salary of $45,000. The company plans to make the following changes:
- Hire 10 new employees in Department A, each with a starting salary of $55,000.
- Transfer 5 employees from Department B to Department C. Each transferred employee earns the mean salary of Department B ($60,000) and keeps this salary.
- Lay off 15 employees from Department C whose mean salary was $40,000.
- Apply a 5% raise to all remaining employees in Department B.
Calculate the new mean salary of all employees in the company after these changes. Round your answer to the nearest integer.

A rectangular box has integer side lengths a, b, c (all positive). Its space diagonal has length sqrt(50), its surface area is 2(ab + bc + ca) = 94, and the sum of the three edge lengths is a + b + c = 12. Find the ordered triple (a, b, c) with a ≤ b ≤ c.

A warehouse has three types of boxes: Small (S), Medium (M), and Large (L). Initially, the ratio of S:M:L is 5:3:2, and there are 1200 boxes in total. Then, the following events occur:
1. A shipment increases the number of Small boxes by 20% and the number of Medium boxes by 15%.
2. 40 Large boxes are shipped out of the warehouse.
3. 10% of the new total number of Small boxes are repackaged and converted into Medium boxes (one Small box becomes one Medium box).
What is the final number of Medium boxes in the warehouse?
Compute the value of the sum S = Σ_{k=1}^{2024} 1 / [k(k+1)]. Express S as a reduced fraction p/q.

A training track is in the shape of a circle with a radius of 200 meters. Runner A starts at point P and runs clockwise at a constant speed of 5 meters per second. Runner B starts at the same point P at the same time and runs counterclockwise at a constant speed of 3 meters per second. When they meet for the fourth time (not counting their start at point P), how many meters has Runner A run? Use 3.14 for pi.

A circle of radius 5 is inscribed in an isosceles trapezoid whose parallel sides have lengths 16 (long base) and 6 (short base). The circle is tangent to both bases and to both non-parallel legs. Compute the exact area of the trapezoid.

An investment portfolio of $100,000 is allocated among three assets: Stocks (60%), Bonds (30%), and Cash (10%). Over the course of one year, Stocks gain 12%, Bonds gain 4%, and Cash gains 1%. At the end of the year, the investor rebalances the portfolio back to its original target allocation percentages (60% Stocks, 30% Bonds, 10% Cash). How much money (in dollars) must be transferred out of the Stocks asset to achieve this rebalancing?

In triangle ABC, the side lengths are a = BC = 13, b = CA = 14, c = AB = 15. Let M be the midpoint of BC. Compute the length AM (the median from A to BC). Express your answer in the form (1/2)·√n, where n is a positive integer.

Uma fábrica produz três tipos de peças: A, B e C. A proporção de produção diária entre A, B e C é de 4:3:3, respectivamente. A fábrica produz um total de 2000 peças por dia. No controle de qualidade, constatou-se que 2% das peças A, 4% das peças B e 5% das peças C produzidas apresentam defeitos. Qual é o número total de peças sem defeito produzidas pela fábrica em um dia?

Una caja rectangular tiene lados enteros positivos a, b y c. Su diagonal espacial mide √49, su área superficial es 2(ab + bc + ca) = 72, y la suma de las aristas es a + b + c = 11. Encuentra la terna ordenada (a, b, c) con a ≤ b ≤ c.
A committee of 5 people is to be chosen from a group of 7 men and 6 women. How many different committees can be formed that contain at least 3 women?

A cryptographer designs a 4-round Feistel-like block cipher on 64-bit blocks. In each round, the right half is transformed by a keyed function F_k(x) = (a·x + b) mod 2^32, where a and b are 32-bit round keys with a odd. The round operation is: new_left = right; new_right = left XOR F_k(right). After 4 rounds, the final left and right halves are concatenated (no final swap). Suppose the round keys for the four rounds are (a1,b1)=(7, 0x9E3779B9), (a2,b2)=(5, 0x243F6A88), (a3,b3)=(9, 0xB7E15162), (a4,b4)=(11, 0xC0CF3B28). Encrypt the 64-bit plaintext block whose left half is 0x01234567 and right half is 0x89ABCDEF. Give the resulting 64-bit ciphertext as a single hexadecimal value (prefix 0x).

A farmer plans to cultivate wheat and barley. It costs $40 per acre to grow wheat and $60 per acre to grow barley. The water requirement is 300 gallons per acre for wheat and 200 gallons per acre for barley. If the farmer wants to fully spend a budget of $5,600 and fully utilize exactly 29,000 gallons of water, how many acres of wheat must they plant?

A cryptographer designs a 12-character password from an alphabet of 26 lowercase letters, 10 digits, and 14 special symbols. The password must contain at least one character from each of the three classes, no character may appear more than once, and the first and last characters must both be digits. How many distinct valid passwords are possible?

A rectangular garden is surrounded by a uniform concrete path that is 2 meters wide. The area of the garden alone is 120 square meters, and the total area of the garden and the path combined is 224 square meters. What is the perimeter (in meters) of the garden alone?
Compute the determinant of the 3x3 matrix A = [[2,1,0],[1,3,1],[0,1,2]].

An auditorium has 30 rows of seats. The first row has 20 seats, and each successive row has 2 more seats than the row before it. During a concert, 80% of the seats in the first 15 rows are occupied, and 60% of the seats in the remaining 15 rows are occupied. What is the total number of occupied seats in the auditorium?
Find the smallest positive integer n such that n is divisible by 7, n+1 is divisible by 8, and n+2 is divisible by 9.

Uma loja de eletrônicos vende computadores, tablets e celulares. No início de um mês, o estoque total era de 300 aparelhos, dos quais 15% eram computadores, 35% eram tablets e o restante eram celulares. Ao longo do mês, a loja vendeu 40% dos computadores, 60% dos tablets e 50% dos celulares de seu estoque. Quantos aparelhos restaram no estoque ao final desse mês?
Calcule el valor exacto de la suma S = 1 + 2 + 3 + ... + 100.

A logistics company manages three warehouses: A, B, and C. Warehouse A has 120 tons of cargo, Warehouse B has 80 tons, and Warehouse C has 150 tons. To balance the load, cargo is redistributed in the following sequence:
1. First, 15% of Warehouse A's cargo is moved to Warehouse B.
2. Second, 20% of Warehouse C's cargo is moved to Warehouse B.
3. Third, 25% of the current cargo in Warehouse B is moved to Warehouse A.
4. Fourth, Warehouse C ships 30 tons of its cargo to an external store.
5. Finally, Warehouse A receives an additional 15 tons from an external shipment.
Let $W_A$, $W_B$, and $W_C$ be the final cargo amounts (in tons) in warehouses A, B, and C, respectively. What is the value of $W_A + 2 W_B - W_C$?

A rectangular box has integer side lengths a, b, c (all positive). Its volume is 180 cubic units and its surface area is 208 square units. Additionally, the longest edge is exactly 3 units longer than the shortest edge. Determine the ordered triple (a, b, c) with a ≤ b ≤ c that satisfies all three conditions. Give the three integers separated by commas.

A sequence of integers $a_1, a_2, a_3, \dots$ is defined by the recurrence relation $a_1 = 3$, $a_2 = 7$, and $a_n = 3a_{n-1} - 2a_{n-2}$ for all $n \ge 3$. Find the remainder when $a_{100}$ is divided by 1000.
Let f(x) = x^3 - 6x^2 + 11x - 6. Compute the sum of all distinct real values of x for which f(x) = 0. Give a single number.

A bag contains 5 red marbles, 4 blue marbles, and 3 green marbles. Three marbles are drawn at random from the bag without replacement. Let $P$ be the probability that at least two of the drawn marbles have the same color. If $P$ is written as a simplified fraction $a/b$, what is the value of $a + b$?
A circle of radius 5 is inscribed in an isosceles trapezoid whose parallel sides have lengths 14 and 6. Compute the area of the trapezoid. Give a single number.

In a right triangle $ABC$ with the right angle at $B$, the hypotenuse $AC$ has a length of 25. The sum of the lengths of the two legs $AB$ and $BC$ is 35. A square is inscribed inside the triangle such that one of its vertices coincides with $B$, the opposite vertex lies on the hypotenuse $AC$, and the other two vertices lie on the legs $AB$ and $BC$. If the side length of the square is written as a simplified fraction $p/q$, what is the value of $p+q$?
A sequence is defined by a_1 = 2 and a_{n+1} = 3a_n - 2 for n ≥ 1. Compute a_6. Give a single integer.

Considere uma progressão aritmética $A = (a_1, a_2, \dots, a_{100})$ com o primeiro termo $a_1 = 7$ e a razão $d = 4$. Considere também uma progressão geométrica $B = (b_1, b_2, \dots, b_{10})$ com o primeiro termo $b_1 = 3$ e a razão comum $q = 2$. Seja $S_A$ a soma de todos os termos da progressão aritmética $A$, e seja $S_B$ a soma de todos os termos da progressão geométrica $B$. Calcule o valor da diferença $S_A - S_B$.
Una caja rectangular tiene lados enteros positivos a, b y c. Su volumen es 120 cm³ y su área superficial es 198 cm². Halle el valor de a + b + c, donde a ≤ b ≤ c. Dé un solo número entero.

A delivery truck must visit five warehouses in the following sequence: A, B, C, D, and E. The baseline distances between the successive warehouses are:
- A to B: 12.5 km
- B to C: 8.2 km
- C to D: 15.4 km
- D to E: 9.1 km

However, the truck encounters a detour between C and D that adds exactly 3.5 km to that segment, and another detour between D and E that adds 1.2 km.

The fuel consumption rate of the truck depends on its payload weight:
- It consumes 0.15 liters of fuel per kilometer when the payload is strictly less than 5.0 tons.
- It consumes 0.22 liters of fuel per kilometer when the payload is 5.0 tons or more.

The payload weight changes at each warehouse as follows:
- The truck starts at warehouse A with a payload of 6.4 tons.
- At warehouse B, 2.1 tons of payload are unloaded.
- At warehouse C, 1.8 tons of payload are unloaded.
- At warehouse D, 0.5 tons of payload are loaded.

Calculate the total volume of fuel in liters consumed by the truck for the entire journey from A to E. Provide only the final decimal number, rounded to two decimal places if necessary.
A right circular cone has base radius 3 and slant height 5. Compute its volume. Give a single number (you may leave π in your answer).

An investor deposits $15,000 into a financial fund. The investment grows over a three-year period under the following conditions:
- Year 1: An annual interest rate of 6% compounded semi-annually. At the end of Year 1, an annual administration fee of $120 is deducted from the balance (after interest is compounded).
- Year 2: An annual interest rate of 8% compounded quarterly. At the end of Year 2, an annual administration fee of $120 is deducted from the balance (after interest is compounded).
- Year 3: An annual interest rate of 5% compounded monthly. At the end of Year 3, an annual administration fee of $120 is deducted from the balance (after interest is compounded).

At the end of Year 3, after all interest has been compounded and fees deducted, a tax of 15% is applied to the net gain. The net gain is defined as the final balance minus the initial deposit of $15,000.

Calculate the final value of the investment at the end of the three-year period after the tax is deducted. Provide only the final number rounded to two decimal places (the nearest cent).
How many ordered pairs of positive integers (m, n) satisfy m^2 + n^2 = 65? Give a single nonnegative integer.

A security code consists of a sequence of 6 characters satisfying the following constraints:
1. The first two characters must be distinct uppercase letters chosen from the set {A, B, C, D, E, F}.
2. The next three characters must be digits chosen from the set {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7}. The digits do not need to be distinct, but their sum must be exactly 10.
3. The last character must be a special symbol chosen from the set {@, #, $, &}.
4. The security code cannot contain both the letter 'A' and the digit '1'.

Calculate the total number of unique valid security codes that can be formed under these rules. Provide only the final integer.
A triangle has vertices at A = (0, 0), B = (6, 0), and C = (2, 4). Compute the length of side BC. Give a single number.

A sequence $a_n$ is defined recursively as follows:
$a_1 = 3$
$a_2 = 5$
$a_n = 2a_{n-1} - a_{n-2} + (-1)^n \cdot n$ for all $n \ge 3$.

Calculate the value of $a_{10}$. Provide only the final integer.
Compute the determinant of the 3×3 matrix [[2, 1, 3], [1, 0, 2], [4, 1, 5]]. Give a single integer.

Um baralho especial contém 40 cartas numeradas de 1 a 40. Três cartas são retiradas simultaneamente e ao acaso desse baralho. Determine o número total de maneiras distintas de escolher essas três cartas de modo que a soma dos três números escritos nelas seja um múltiplo de 3. Forneça apenas o número inteiro final correspondente ao total de maneiras.
Calcule el valor de la suma 1 + 2 + 3 + ... + 100. Dé un solo número entero.

Lee el siguiente texto en español y produce un resumen breve (máximo 60 palabras) que contenga únicamente las dos ideas principales y omita cualquier detalle numérico o estadístico.

Texto:
"La migración rural hacia las ciudades ha transformado profundamente la estructura social de América Latina durante el último siglo. Históricamente, las comunidades agrícolas sostenían economías de subsistencia basadas en el trabajo familiar y el intercambio local. Con la industrialización, millones de personas se desplazaron hacia centros urbanos en busca de empleo formal y acceso a servicios como educación y salud. Este fenómeno generó el crecimiento acelerado de barrios periféricos, conocidos como asentamientos informales, donde las condiciones de vivienda suelen ser precarias. Además, produjo cambios culturales significativos: la pérdida de lenguas indígenas, la transformación de las dietas tradicionales y la modificación de las estructuras familiares. Las políticas públicas han intentado responder con programas de vivienda social y regularización de tierras, aunque los resultados han sido desiguales según el país. Expertos señalan que, sin inversión sostenida en infraestructura urbana y servicios básicos, la desigualdad entre zonas rurales y urbanas seguirá ampliándose en las próximas décadas."

Read the text below and summarize the milestones of the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) for the years 1996, 2002, and 2021.

Constraints:
1. It must consist of exactly three bullet points, in chronological order of the three years (1996, 2002, 2021).
2. Each bullet point must start with the year followed by a colon (e.g., '1996: [milestone]').
3. Do not include any information about the Hubble Space Telescope.

Text:
The Hubble Space Telescope was launched in 1990 into low Earth orbit. In 1993, the first servicing mission corrected its flawed mirror. Meanwhile, plans for a successor began. The Next Generation Space Telescope project started in 1996. In 2002, this project was renamed the James Webb Space Telescope. Construction of the telescope's main components was completed in late 2016. After extensive testing, the telescope was launched on an Ariane 5 rocket on December 25, 2021. Hubble continued operating, receiving its last servicing mission in 2009.

Read the following short article and produce a summary that meets the stated constraint.

Article:
"The James Webb Space Telescope, launched in December 2021, is the largest optical telescope in space. It operates primarily in the infrared spectrum and orbits the Sun near the second Lagrange point (L2), about 1.5 million kilometers from Earth. Its segmented primary mirror is 6.5 meters in diameter, made of gold-coated beryllium segments that can fold for launch. Webb is a collaboration between NASA, the European Space Agency (ESA), and the Canadian Space Agency (CSA). Its mission objectives include observing the first galaxies that formed after the Big Bang, studying the formation and evolution of galaxies, understanding star and planetary system formation, and examining exoplanet atmospheres for biosignatures. The telescope succeeded the Hubble Space Telescope as NASA's flagship astrophysics mission. Its sunshield, the size of a tennis court, keeps the instruments at cryogenic temperatures around 50 Kelvin. The first deep-field image, released in July 2022, showed the galaxy cluster SMACS 0723 as it appeared 4.6 billion years ago."

Constraint: Write a single-sentence summary of no more than 30 words that names the telescope, its primary operating spectrum, its launch year, and its lead space agency.

Based on the historical text below, summarize the development of penicillin by Alexander Fleming, Howard Florey, and Ernst Chain.

Constraints:
1. It must contain exactly two paragraphs: the first paragraph summarizing Alexander Fleming's contribution, and the second paragraph summarizing Howard Florey and Ernst Chain's contribution.
2. Do not mention Louis Pasteur or rabies.
3. The total summary must not exceed 80 words.

Text:
In 1885, Louis Pasteur successfully used his rabies vaccine on a human patient, a major breakthrough in immunology. Decades later, in 1928, Alexander Fleming returned to his laboratory at St. Mary's Hospital in London and noticed that a mold called Penicillium notatum had contaminated a petri dish of Staphylococci, preventing the bacteria's growth. Fleming published his findings on penicillin, but could not isolate the active substance in large quantities. In 1938, Howard Florey and Ernst Chain at Oxford University began researching penicillin. By 1940, they purified it and demonstrated its effectiveness in mice, leading to mass production during World War II.

Read the following short article and produce a summary that meets the stated constraint.

Article:
"The Voyager 1 spacecraft, launched on September 5, 1977, from Cape Canaveral aboard a Titan IIIE rocket, is currently the most distant human-made object from Earth. It visited Jupiter in 1979 and Saturn in 1980, returning the first detailed images of those planets and their moons. After completing its planetary mission, Voyager 1 was redirected onto a trajectory that took it out of the ecliptic plane. On August 25, 2012, it crossed the heliopause and entered interstellar space, becoming the first spacecraft to do so. As of 2024, it is over 24 billion kilometers from the Sun and continues to transmit data from its three operating instruments. Its sibling, Voyager 2, also launched in 1977, followed a different trajectory that included flybys of Uranus and Neptune. Both spacecraft carry the Voyager Golden Record, a gold-plated copper phonograph record containing sounds and images selected to portray the diversity of life and culture on Earth."

Constraint: Write a two-sentence summary. Sentence 1 must state what Voyager 1 is and when it crossed into interstellar space. Sentence 2 must state what the Golden Record is and which two spacecraft carry it.

Based on the text below, summarize the functions of the Legislative and Judicial branches of the US federal government.

Constraints:
1. The summary must be a single compound sentence.
2. It must follow the exact structure: 'The Legislative branch [function]; the Judicial branch [function].'
3. You must not mention the Executive branch or state governments.

Text:
The United States Constitution establishes three branches of the federal government. The Legislative branch, consisting of Congress, is responsible for making laws and declaring war. The Executive branch, headed by the President, enforces the laws passed by Congress. The Judicial branch, which includes the Supreme Court, interprets the laws and resolves disputes under the Constitution. Meanwhile, state governments also have three branches, but they operate under their respective state constitutions and manage local matters.

Read the following short article and produce a summary that meets the stated constraint.

Article:
"The Rosetta mission was a European Space Agency (ESA) cornerstone mission that rendezvoused with comet 67P/Churyumov-Gerasimenko in August 2014. It consisted of two main elements: the Rosetta orbiter and the Philae lander. Philae was released on November 12, 2014, and made history as the first spacecraft to achieve a soft landing on a comet nucleus, although it landed in a shadowed region that limited its solar power. The orbiter continued to study the comet's nucleus, coma, and tail as the comet approached perihelion in August 2015. Rosetta's instruments measured the ratio of deuterium to hydrogen in the comet's water, finding it significantly higher than Earth's ocean water, which challenged the hypothesis that comets like 67P were the primary source of Earth's water. The mission ended on September 30, 2016, when Rosetta was deliberately crashed into the comet's surface. Data from the mission continued to be analyzed for years afterward, contributing to understanding of cometary composition and the early solar system."

Constraint: Write a three-sentence summary. Sentence 1 must identify the mission and the comet it visited. Sentence 2 must state what Philae accomplished and on what date. Sentence 3 must state the mission's end date and how the mission ended.

Using only the text below, summarize the characteristics of the mantle and the outer core in a table.

Constraints:
1. The table must have exactly two columns: 'Layer' and 'Description'.
2. The table must have exactly two rows of data (one for the mantle, one for the outer core).
3. The description for each layer must specify its physical state (e.g., semi-solid, liquid) and composition based on the text.
4. Do not include the crust, inner core, or any atmospheric layers (like the troposphere or stratosphere).

Text:
Earth has a layered structure. The troposphere is the lowest layer of Earth's atmosphere, containing most of its weather. Below the surface, Earth's crust is solid rock. The mantle, which lies beneath the crust, is composed of semi-solid silicate rocks. Beneath the mantle lies the outer core, which is liquid and composed primarily of iron and nickel. At the center of the Earth is the solid inner core, made of iron and nickel. The stratosphere is above the troposphere.

Article:
"The Hubble Space Telescope was launched into low Earth orbit on April 24, 1990, aboard the Space Shuttle Discovery during mission STS-31. It was deployed the following day and remains operational as of 2024. Hubble's primary mirror is 2.4 meters in diameter, and it observes primarily in ultraviolet, visible, and near-infrared wavelengths. Shortly after launch, a flaw in the primary mirror's optics was discovered, causing spherical aberration; this was corrected during the first servicing mission in 1993 by installing corrective optics (COSTAR). Five subsequent servicing missions, conducted between 1997 and 2009, replaced instruments and upgraded systems, greatly extending the telescope's scientific capabilities. Hubble has contributed to numerous major discoveries, including measuring the rate of expansion of the universe (leading to the 2011 Nobel Prize in Physics for the discovery of accelerating expansion), observing distant supernovae, and capturing the Hubble Deep Field images that revealed thousands of galaxies in a small patch of sky. Its successor, the James Webb Space Telescope, was launched in December 2021."

Constraint: Write a summary of exactly 40 to 50 words that mentions the launch date, the mirror diameter, the 1993 servicing mission correction, and at least one scientific contribution.

Com base no texto abaixo, resuma a expedição de Pedro Álvares Cabral.

Restrições:
1. Deve ser escrita em apenas uma única frase em português.
2. Deve conter a data de partida de Lisboa, o nome do capitão da armada e a data de chegada ao Brasil.
3. Não deve mencionar Vasco da Gama ou a Índia.

Texto:
Em 1497, Vasco da Gama partiu de Lisboa rumo à Índia, contornando o Cabo da Boa Esperança e estabelecendo uma nova rota comercial em 1498. Poucos anos depois, em 9 de março de 1500, a armada liderada por Pedro Álvares Cabral partiu de Lisboa com destino às Índias. No entanto, a frota afastou-se do continente africano e, em 22 de abril de 1500, avistou terras que viriam a ser o Brasil, aportando no atual estado da Bahia.

Lee el siguiente artículo breve y produce un resumen que cumpla con la restricción indicada.

Artículo:
"La misión Cassini-Huygens fue una colaboración entre la NASA, la Agencia Espacial Europea (ESA) y la Agencia Espacial Italiana (ASI) para estudiar Saturno y su sistema de anillos y lunas. La nave Cassini fue lanzada el 15 de octubre de 1997 y entró en órbita alrededor de Saturno el 1 de julio de 2004. La sonda Huygens, parte de la misión, se separó de Cassini el 25 de diciembre de 2004 y descendió sobre Titán, la luna más grande de Saturno, el 14 de enero de 2005, convirtiéndose en la primera sonda en aterrizar en un cuerpo del sistema solar exterior. Durante sus 13 años en órbita, Cassini realizó descubrimientos importantes, incluyendo la detección de géiseres de agua en Encélado y la observación de lagos de hidrocarburos líquidos en Titán. Its mission objectives include observing the first galaxies that formed in the early universe, studying the formation and evolution of galaxies, and examining the birth of stars and planetary systems. Over the following decades, four additional servicing missions (in 1997, 1999, 2002, and 2009) upgraded Hubble's instruments and extended its operational life. Hubble has made numerous scientific contributions, including helping to determine the rate of expansion of the universe, observing distant galaxies and nebulae, and providing high-resolution images of planets within the Solar System. As of 2024, Hubble remains operational and continues to work alongside the James Webb Space Telescope."

"La misión Cassini-Huygens fue una colaboración entre la NASA, la Agencia Espacial Europea (ESA) y la Agencia Espacial Italiana (ASI) para estudiar Saturno y su sistema de anillos y lunas. La nave Cassini fue lanzada el 15 de octubre de 1997 a bordo de un cohete Titan IV-B desde Cabo Cañaveral. Tras un viaje de casi siete años que incluyó sobrevuelos de Venus y Júpiter, Cassini entró en órbita alrededor de Saturno el 1 de julio de 2004. El 25 de diciembre de 2004, la sonda Huygens se separó de Cassini y descendió hacia Titán, la luna más grande de Saturno, convirtiéndose el 14 de enero de 2005 en el primer objeto construido por el ser humano en aterrizar en un satélite natural del Sistema Solar exterior. Cassini continuó estudiando Saturno durante trece años, realizando descubrimientos sobre la estructura de los anillos y la posible habitabilidad de la luna Encélado. El 15 de septiembre de 2017, la misión terminó con una inmersión controlada de Cassini en la atmósfera de Saturno para evitar la contaminación biológica de las lunas potencialmente habitables."

Restricciones:
- Produce exactamente dos oraciones.
- La oración 1 debe identificar las agencias participantes y el objetivo principal de la misión.

Based on the text below, provide a summary of the Explorer 1 satellite that adheres strictly to the following constraints:
1. The summary must consist of exactly three sentences.
2. The first sentence must state the launch date of Explorer 1 and its launch vehicle.
3. The second sentence must identify the primary scientific instrument it carried, its designer, and the major discovery made.
4. The third sentence must state when Explorer 1 re-entered Earth's atmosphere.
5. Do not mention Project Vanguard, Vanguard 1, Juno II, or Pioneer 3 in your summary.

Text:
Project Vanguard was the second satellite program proposed by the United States, managed by the Naval Research Laboratory. Vanguard 1 was launched on March 17, 1958, and remains the oldest artificial satellite still in orbit. It was powered by solar cells and measured the geoid of the Earth.
Explorer 1 was the first satellite launched by the United States, sent into orbit on January 31, 1958, from Cape Canaveral. It was launched aboard a Juno I rocket, which was a variant of the Jupiter-C exploratory rocket. Explorer 1 carried a cosmic ray detector designed by Dr. James Van Allen of the State University of Iowa. The data returned by this instrument led to the discovery of a zone of high-radiation particles trapped by Earth's magnetic field, now known as the Van Allen radiation belt. Explorer 1 stopped transmitting data on May 23, 1958, when its batteries died, but it remained in orbit for over twelve years before re-entering Earth's atmosphere and burning up on March 31, 1970.
The Juno II was a different launch vehicle used for later space probes like Pioneer 3 and Pioneer 4 in late 1958 and 1959. Pioneer 3, launched on December 6, 1958, failed to reach orbit but discovered a second outer radiation belt.

Read the following passage and produce a summary that satisfies ALL of the constraints listed below.

CONSTRAINTS:
1. The summary must be exactly 2 sentences long.
2. The summary must mention the year 1859, the term "natural selection," and the phrase "On the Origin of Species."
3. The summary must NOT mention Alfred Russel Wallace, the Linnean Society meeting, or any religious objections.
4. The summary must emphasize the mechanism of variation and inheritance rather than the book's commercial success.

PASSAGE:
"On the Origin of Species," published in 1859 by Charles Darwin, introduced the theory of evolution by natural selection to a wide audience. The book argued that species change over time through a process in which individuals with heritable variations that are better suited to their environment are more likely to survive and reproduce. Darwin drew on observations from his voyage on HMS Beagle, experiments with domesticated animals, and the fossil record to support his claims. Although the book sold out its first printing on the day of publication and went through multiple editions, its lasting importance lies in the mechanism it proposed: that small, heritable variations accumulate across generations under the pressure of environmental competition. The idea was not entirely new; Alfred Russel Wallace had independently arrived at a similar theory, and a joint paper by Darwin and Wallace was read at the Linnean Society in 1858. The book also drew criticism from religious thinkers who objected to its implications for the origin of humanity, but Darwin largely avoided theological debate in the text itself, focusing instead on variation, inheritance, and the struggle for existence.

Provide only the 2-sentence summary.

Based on the provided text, write a summary focusing only on the discoveries of Nihonium and Tennessine. Your summary must satisfy the following constraints:
1. The summary must be exactly two sentences long.
2. The first sentence must state the atomic number, official name, discovering institution (or team leader), and the colliding isotopes used to synthesize Nihonium.
3. The second sentence must state the atomic number, official name, the four collaborating institutions, and the colliding isotopes used to synthesize Tennessine.
4. Do not include any information about Moscovium or Oganesson.

Text:
In December 2015, the International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC) and the International Union of Pure and Applied Physics (IUPAP) recognized the discovery of four new chemical elements. Element 113 was officially named Nihonium (Nh). It was discovered by a team at the RIKEN Nishina Center for Accelerator-Based Science in Japan, led by Kōsuke Morita. The team synthesized it by colliding bismuth-209 with zinc-70 in 2004 and 2012. Element 115 was named Moscovium (Mc). It was discovered by a joint collaboration of the Joint Institute for Nuclear Research (JINR) in Dubna, Russia, and the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory (LLNL) in California, USA. Element 117 was named Tennessine (Ts). It was discovered by a collaboration including JINR, LLNL, Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL), and Vanderbilt University. It was synthesized by colliding berkelium-249 with calcium-48. Element 118 was named Oganesson (Og), honoring Yuri Oganessian. It was synthesized by colliding californium-249 with calcium-48 by a collaboration of JINR and LLNL.

Read the following passage and produce a summary that satisfies ALL of the constraints listed below.

CONSTRAINTS:
1. The summary must be exactly 3 sentences long.
2. The summary must mention the Apollo 11 mission, the year 1969, and the Sea of Tranquility.
3. The summary must NOT mention Michael Collins, the Saturn V rocket, or the cost of the program.
4. The summary must emphasize the scientific significance of the lunar samples rather than the geopolitical competition with the Soviet Union.

PASSAGE:
Apollo 11, the mission that landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969, marked a defining moment in the history of space exploration. On July 20, Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin descended in the Lunar Module Eagle to the Sea of Tranquility, a relatively smooth basaltic plain chosen for its safe landing conditions, while Michael Collins remained in lunar orbit aboard the Command Module Columbia. The astronauts spent about two and a half hours on the surface collecting lunar samples, deploying experiments, and planting a flag. They returned roughly 21.5 kilograms of lunar material, including basalts, breccias, and regolith, which scientists on Earth analyzed for years. These samples revealed the Moon's composition, age, and the lack of volatile elements, providing the first direct evidence of its geological history and constraining models of its origin. The mission was launched on the Saturn V rocket from Kennedy Space Center and was a direct response to the geopolitical competition with the Soviet Union during the Cold War, though the scientific return from the samples has proven far more enduring than the political context that produced the mission.

Provide only the 3-sentence summary.

Based on the provided text, write a summary comparing only the transport methods, arrival dates at the South Pole, and the ultimate fates of the two polar parties. Your summary must satisfy the following constraints:
1. The summary must consist of exactly three sentences.
2. The first sentence must describe Roald Amundsen's expedition, including their transport method, the date they reached the South Pole, and their fate (survival).
3. The second sentence must describe Robert Falcon Scott's expedition, including their primary transport methods (ponies, motor sledges, dogs, man-hauling), the date they reached the South Pole, and their tragic fate.
4. The third sentence must state the base camp locations of both expeditions (Framheim and Cape Evans).
5. Do not mention the names of the ships used by either expedition.

Text:
In the early 20th century, the race to the South Pole was contested between Roald Amundsen's Norwegian expedition and Robert Falcon Scott's British Terra Nova expedition. Amundsen's expedition sailed on the ship Fram. They established their base camp at Framheim in the Bay of Whales in January 1911. Amundsen's team used Greenland sled dogs for transport. They reached the South Pole on December 14, 1911, and named their polar base Polheim. All five members of the polar party returned safely to their base camp on January 26, 1912. Scott's Terra Nova expedition established its base camp at Cape Evans on Ross Island in January 1911. Scott's team used a combination of Siberian ponies, motorized sledges, and dogs, but ultimately relied heavily on man-hauling. Scott and his four companions reached the South Pole on January 17, 1912, only to find Amundsen's Norwegian flag. Tragically, all five members of Scott's polar party died on the return journey due to starvation, exhaustion, and extreme cold.

Read the following passage and produce a summary that satisfies ALL of the constraints listed below.

CONSTRAINTS:
1. The summary must be exactly 2 sentences long.
2. The summary must mention the Haber-Bosch process, the year 1909, and the element nitrogen.
3. The summary must NOT mention Fritz Haber's later work on chemical weapons, the BASF company, or the energy intensity of the process.
4. The summary must emphasize the role of the process in enabling synthetic fertilizer production rather than its industrial scale.

PASSAGE:
The Haber-Bosch process, first demonstrated at a workable scale by Fritz Haber in 1909 and later industrialized by Carl Bosch, made it possible to fix atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia on an industrial scale. By combining nitrogen and hydrogen under high temperature and pressure with an iron catalyst, the process yields ammonia, which can be converted into fertilizers such as ammonium nitrate and urea. Before Haber's work, the main source of fixed nitrogen for agriculture was guano and Chilean saltpeter, both of which were limited and increasingly expensive. The ability to produce ammonia from the unlimited nitrogen in the atmosphere transformed global agriculture and is widely credited with enabling the food supply that supports roughly half of the world's current population. The process was developed in partnership with the BASF company, which built the first industrial plant at Oppau, Germany. Haber himself later became a controversial figure for his direction of chemical weapons research during World War I, though the agricultural legacy of his earlier nitrogen-fixation work has had a far larger and more lasting impact on humanity.

Provide only the 2-sentence summary.

Based on the provided text, write a summary comparing the Treaty of Versailles and the Treaty of Trianon under the following constraints:
1. The summary must consist of exactly three sentences.
2. The first sentence must state the signing date, signing location, and target country of the Treaty of Versailles, as well as the name of its specific clause concerning responsibility.
3. The second sentence must state the signing date, signing location, and target country of the Treaty of Trianon, as well as the percentage of territory lost by that country.
4. The third sentence must state the financial reparations amount for Germany and the army size limit for Hungary.
5. Do not mention the Treaty of Saint-Germain-en-Laye or Austria.

Text:
Following World War I, several peace treaties were signed between the Allied powers and the defeated Central Powers. The Treaty of Versailles was signed on June 28, 1919, in the Hall of Mirrors at the Palace of Versailles. It dealt specifically with Germany. Under Article 231, also known as the 'War Guilt Clause,' Germany accepted sole responsibility for causing the war. The treaty required Germany to disarm, make substantial territorial concessions, and pay massive financial reparations, initially set at 132 billion gold marks. The Treaty of Saint-Germain-en-Laye was signed on September 10, 1919, establishing the Republic of Austria and banning political union with Germany. The Treaty of Trianon was signed on June 4, 1920, at the Grand Trianon palace in Versailles. It regulated the status of the independent Hungarian state and defined its borders. Under this treaty, Hungary lost 72% of its pre-war territory and 64% of its population to neighboring states, including Romania, Czechoslovakia, and the Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes. It also limited the Hungarian army to 35,000 officers and men.

Read the following passage and produce a summary that satisfies ALL of the constraints listed below.

CONSTRAINTS:
1. The summary must be exactly 3 sentences long.
2. The summary must mention the Rosetta Stone, the year 1799, and the Ptolemaic dynasty.
3. The summary must NOT mention Napoleon Bonaparte, the Battle of the Nile, or the British Museum's current location.
4. The summary must emphasize the linguistic content of the inscription rather than the political circumstances of its discovery.

PASSAGE:
The Rosetta Stone, discovered in 1799 by French soldiers during renovations at a fort near the town of Rosetta (Rashid) in the Nile Delta, is a granodiorite stele inscribed with a single decree issued in 196 BCE during the Ptolemaic dynasty. The decree is written in three scripts: ancient Greek, Demotic Egyptian, and Egyptian hieroglyphs. Because the Greek text could be readily read, the stone provided the key that allowed scholars, most famously Jean-François Champollion in 1822, to decipher Egyptian hieroglyphs for the first time. The inscription itself concerns a tax relief granted to the priesthood of Ptolemy V, but its lasting importance lies in the parallel texts, which made it possible to recover the structure and vocabulary of a writing system that had been unreadable for more than a millennium. The stone changed hands several times after its discovery: it was taken by the British after the surrender of French forces following the Battle of the Nile, and it has been on display at the British Museum in London since 1802. The political and military context of its transfer has been the subject of long-running repatriation requests, but the stone's scientific and linguistic value rests entirely on the three-script decree it carries.

Provide only the 3-sentence summary.

Com base no texto fornecido, escreva um resumo focando apenas nas expedições de Vasco da Gama e Pedro Álvares Cabral sob as seguintes restrições:
1. O resumo deve conter exatamente três frases.
2. A primeira frase deve detalhar a expedição de Vasco da Gama, mencionando a data de partida de Lisboa, a data de chegada a Calecute e o número de navios em sua frota.
3. A segunda frase deve detalhar a expedição de Pedro Álvares Cabral, mencionando a data de partida, a data de chegada ao Brasil e o número de navios em sua frota.
4. A terceira frase deve mencionar o nome original dado ao Brasil por Cabral (Ilha de Vera Cruz).
5. Não mencione Bartolomeu Dias ou o Cabo da Boa Esperança.

Texto:
Durante a Era dos Descobrimentos, os navegadores portugueses realizaram expedições históricas cruciais para o mapeamento global. Bartolomeu Dias foi o primeiro a contornar o Cabo da Boa Esperança (anteriormente chamado Cabo das Tormentas) em fevereiro de 1488, abrindo caminho para o Oceano Índico. Ele viajou com duas caravelas de 50 toneladas. Vasco da Gama liderou a primeira expedição a chegar à Índia por mar, partindo de Lisboa em 8 de julho de 1497 com quatro navios (São Gabriel, São Rafael, Bérrio e um navio de mantimentos). Ele chegou a Calecute em 20 de maio de 1498, estabelecendo a Rota do Cabo. Pedro Álvares Cabral comandou uma frota de 13 navios que partiu em 9 de março de 1500. Embora o destino final fosse a Índia, a frota desviou-se para o oeste e chegou ao Brasil em 22 de abril de 1500, ancorando em Porto Seguro. Ele declarou a terra propriedade da coroa portuguesa, chamando-a de Ilha de Vera Cruz.

Lee el siguiente pasaje y produce un resumen que cumpla TODAS las restricciones indicadas a continuación.

RESTRICCIONES:
1. El resumen debe tener exactamente 2 oraciones.
2. El resumen debe mencionar la Revolución Francesa, el año 1789, y la Declaración de los Derechos del Hombre y del Ciudadano.
3. El resumen NO debe mencionar a Maximilien Robespierre, el calendario republicano, ni la ejecución de Luis XVI.
4. El resumen debe enfatizar el contenido de los derechos declarados más que las fases políticas de la revolución.

PASO:
La Revolución Francesa, que comenzó en 1789 con la toma de la Bastilla el 14 de julio, transformó profundamente la política y la sociedad europeas. En agosto de ese mismo año, la Asamblea Nacional Constituyente aprobó la Declaración de los Derechos del Hombre y del Ciudadano, un documento que proclamaba derechos universales como la libertad, la igualdad, la propiedad y la seguridad, y que establecía que la soberanía residía en la nación y no en el monarca. La Declaración se inspiró en las ideas de los philosophes de la Ilustración, en particular de Locke, Rousseau y Montesquieu, y sirvió como preámbulo ideológico de las constituciones que siguieron. La revolución atravesó varias fases políticas, incluyendo el período del Terror dirigido por Maximilien Robespierre, la instauración de un calendario republicano que reemplazó el calendario gregoriano, y la ejecución de Luis XVI en 1793. Sin embargo, el legado más duradero de la Revolución Francesa no se encuentra en estos episodios políticos, sino en la afirmación de derechos universales que han influido en las constituciones democráticas de todo el mundo hasta hoy.

Proporciona solo el resumen de 2 oraciones.

Read the following passage and produce a single-sentence summary of at most 35 words that captures the central claim, the main supporting evidence, and the principal limitation acknowledged by the authors. Do not include any information that is not present in the passage.

Passage:
"In a 2021 randomized controlled trial, Chen and colleagues reported that a six-week program of twice-weekly 30-minute sessions of moderate-intensity aerobic exercise significantly reduced self-reported anxiety scores on the GAD-7 scale in 142 sedentary adults diagnosed with generalized anxiety disorder, compared with a waitlist control (mean difference -4.2 points, 95% CI -5.8 to -2.6, p<0.001). Secondary outcomes showed modest improvements in sleep quality (PSQI reduction of 1.9 points) but no statistically significant change in depressive symptoms on the PHQ-9. The authors noted that participants were recruited from a single urban university clinic, that adherence was self-reported rather than objectively measured, and that the trial did not include an active comparator such as cognitive behavioral therapy, limiting generalizability and the ability to attribute effects specifically to aerobic exercise rather than to structured social contact or attention from study staff."

Read the following passage and produce a single-sentence summary of at most 35 words that states the main finding, the mechanism proposed by the authors, and the key methodological weakness identified. Do not include any information that is not present in the passage.

Passage:
"A 2022 observational study by Okafor and colleagues analyzed electronic health records of 18,440 adults with type 2 diabetes across three integrated health systems and found that patients who consistently filled prescriptions for metformin had a 22% lower hazard of a positive PCR test for SARS-CoV-2 during 2020-2021 compared with patients who did not (adjusted HR 0.78, 95% CI 0.69-0.88). The authors proposed that metformin's activation of AMPK and consequent suppression of the mTOR pathway may reduce viral replication in airway epithelium, citing in vitro evidence from earlier work. In the discussion, the authors acknowledged that the study could not rule out residual confounding by health-seeking behavior, because patients who reliably fill chronic-disease prescriptions may also be more likely to practice preventive behaviors such as masking and avoiding crowded indoor settings, and that PCR testing was performed only when clinically indicated rather than through systematic screening."

Passage:
"In a 2023 policy brief, the National Council on Teacher Quality argued that requiring all new K-12 teachers to hold a bachelor's degree in education or in their subject area should be made a uniform national standard, citing as its strongest evidence a 2019 longitudinal study of 2,100 teachers in four U.S. states showing that subject-matched bachelor's preparation was associated with a 0.12 standard deviation gain in student end-of-course exam scores after controlling for school poverty and prior achievement. The brief acknowledged that a 2021 randomized evaluation in Tennessee of 480 alternatively certified teachers found no statistically significant difference in student outcomes compared with traditionally certified peers over a two-year follow-up, and argued that the discrepancy could be explained by differences in study design, sample size, and the specific alternative certification programs evaluated, while critics of the brief maintained that the Tennessee trial, because it was randomized, should weigh more heavily than the observational 2019 study."

Read the following article and produce a single-sentence summary of at most 35 words that captures the main finding, the specific mechanism identified, and the population studied. Do not include background, statistics, or implications.

Article:
A 2024 study published in The Lancet examined the relationship between ultra-processed food (UPF) consumption and cognitive decline in a cohort of 10,775 adults aged 35-74 across eight European countries, followed for an average of 7.2 years. Participants were categorized into quartiles based on UPF intake as a percentage of total energy, using NOVA classification. The study found that individuals in the highest quartile (UPFs comprising more than 32% of daily calories) experienced a 28% faster rate of decline in global cognitive composite scores compared to those in the lowest quartile, after adjusting for age, sex, education, total caloric intake, physical activity, and cardiovascular risk factors. Mediation analysis suggested that approximately 12% of this association was explained by low-grade systemic inflammation, measured via C-reactive protein. The authors proposed that advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) formed during high-temperature industrial processing may cross the blood-brain barrier and trigger neuroinflammatory cascades. A secondary analysis found no significant association between UPF intake and amyloid-beta accumulation measured via PET imaging in a subsample of 1,200 participants, suggesting the cognitive effects may be functional rather than structural. The study's limitations include reliance on self-reported food frequency questionnaires and potential residual confounding by socioeconomic status.

Artículo:
Un estudio de 2024 publicado en The Lancet examinó la relación entre el consumo de alimentos ultraprocesados (UPF) y el deterioro cognitivo en una cohorte de 10.775 adultos de 35 a 74 años en ocho países europeos, seguidos durante una media de 7,2 años. Los participantes fueron categorizados en cuartiles según la ingesta de UPF como porcentaje del total de energía, utilizando la clasificación NOVA. El estudio encontró que los individuos en el cuartil más alto (UPF que comprenden más del 32% de las calorías diarias) experimentaron una tasa de deterioro un 28% más rápida en las puntuaciones cognitivas globales en comparación con los del cuartil más bajo, tras ajustar por edad, sexo, educación, ingesta calórica total, actividad física y factores de riesgo cardiovascular. El análisis de mediación sugirió que aproximadamente el 12% de esta asociación se explicaba por inflamación sistémica de bajo grado, medida mediante proteína C-reactiva. Los autores propusieron que los productos finales de glicación avanzada (AGE) formados durante el procesamiento industrial a alta temperatura podrían cruzar la barrera hematoencefálica y desencadenar cascadas neuroinflamatorias. Un análisis secundario no encontró asociación significativa entre la ingesta de UPF y la acumulación de beta-amiloide medida mediante PET en una submuestra de 1.200 participantes, lo que sugiere que los efectos cognitivos podrían ser funcionales más que estructurales. Passage:
"The 1883 eruption of Krakatoa in the Sunda Strait remains one of the most violent volcanic events in recorded history. The final explosive phase on August 27 ejected an estimated 25 cubic kilometers of rock, ash, and pumice and generated tsunamis that reached heights of up to 40 meters along the coasts of Java and Sumatra. Official Dutch colonial records list 36,417 deaths, though later demographic reconstructions suggest the toll may have been closer to 120,000 when inland displacement, disease, and prolonged starvation are included. Atmospheric effects were global: barographs recorded the pressure wave circling the Earth at least three times, and average Northern Hemisphere temperatures fell by roughly 1.2 °C in the following year. Some historians have argued that the eruption accelerated the decline of the Dutch East Indies trade economy by disrupting shipping lanes for nearly a year, but this claim is disputed by economic historians who point to compensating shifts in trade through Singapore. Modern reassessments using ice-core sulfate layers have largely confirmed the magnitude of the eruption but have revised the death toll downward, not upward, citing earlier overcounting of coastal villages."
