The Polaris Expedition of 1871, commanded by Charles Francis Hall on the USS Polaris, set out to reach the North Pole, reaching a record latitude of 82°11' N. In November 1871, Hall died suddenly after returning from a sledge journey. In October 1872, the expedition split into two. A group of 19 crew members became separated from the ship and drifted on an ice floe for 196 days before being rescued by the sealer Tigress. The remaining 14 crew members stayed aboard the damaged USS Polaris, wintered at Lifeboat Cove, built small boats from the ship's own wooden timbers when the vessel ran aground, and were rescued in June 1873 by the whaler Ravenscraig.

Read the following article and produce a single-sentence summary of at most 30 words that identifies the central claim, the specific dataset used, and the reported accuracy figure. Do not include caveats, methodology, or implications.

Article:
"In a 2022 paper at the Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition, researchers from ETH Zurich introduced 'UrbanTreeNet,' a deep learning model designed to classify tree species from street-level photographs. The model was trained and evaluated on the 'StreetTrees-12K' dataset, which contains 12,480 labeled images covering 28 tree species common to Central European urban environments. On a held-out test set of 2,496 images, UrbanTreeNet achieved a top-1 classification accuracy of 89.4%, outperforming the previous state-of-the-art baseline (ResNet-152 fine-tuned on the same data) by 4.7 percentage points. The authors also reported that performance dropped to 76.1% on images captured under heavy rain or low-light conditions, which they flagged as a limitation. The paper did not report results on non-European tree species."

Read the following text on noble gases and write a summary of exactly two sentences.
Constraint 1: The first sentence must describe the discovery of Argon, naming the discoverers and the chemical/physical action used to isolate it from air.
Constraint 2: The second sentence must describe the discovery of Neon, naming the discoverers and the processes used to isolate it.
Constraint 3: Do not mention Helium, cleveite, or any years.

Text:
The discovery of noble gases began when Pierre Janssen and Norman Lockyer detected helium in the solar spectrum during the 1868 eclipse. Helium was later isolated on Earth in 1895 by William Ramsay from the mineral cleveite. In 1894, Lord Rayleigh and William Ramsay discovered argon by removing all oxygen, nitrogen, and carbon dioxide from a sample of atmospheric air. In 1898, William Ramsay and Morris Travers discovered neon by cooling a sample of air until it liquefied, and then capturing the gas that boiled off first through fractional distillation.

Read the following article and produce a single-sentence summary of at most 35 words that states the main policy outcome, the jurisdiction involved, and the numerical threshold or change described. Do not include political context, stakeholder reactions, or implementation details.

Article:
"On March 14, 2022, the European Parliament voted 339 to 249, with 24 abstentions, to approve a revision of the European Union's Industrial Emissions Directive (IED). The revision lowers the threshold at which livestock farms must comply with the directive's emission-reduction requirements from 400 animal units to 150 animal units for poultry and from 400 to 200 animal units for swine. The revised directive also introduces mandatory monitoring of methane emissions from covered farms and requires farms above the new thresholds to install best-available-technique (BAT) equipment within four years of the directive's entry into force. Member states have until 2026 to transpose the new rules into national law. Environmental groups broadly welcomed the vote, while several agricultural associations expressed concern about compliance costs for medium-sized farms."

Read the text below and write a summary of exactly two sentences.
Constraint 1: The first sentence must state the promotion name of the Rook in Shogi and the unique rule regarding captured pieces in Shogi.
Constraint 2: The second sentence must describe the movement area limitation of the General and the capture mechanism of the Cannon in Xiangqi.
Constraint 3: Do not mention Chess, the King, or the Chariot.

Text:
Traditional board games have varied mechanics for similar pieces. In Chess, the Rook moves orthogonally and can castle with the King. In Shogi, the Rook can promote to a Dragon King when entering the promotion zone, and captured pieces can be dropped back onto the board as one's own. In Xiangqi, the Chariot moves orthogonally like a Rook, the General is restricted to a 3x3 grid called the palace, and the Cannon moves orthogonally but must jump over exactly one piece of any color to make a capture.

Read the following article and produce a single-sentence summary of at most 30 words that identifies the central scientific finding, the astronomical object or system studied, and the reported measurement. Do not include theoretical implications, instrument details, or future work.

Article:
"A team of astronomers using the James Webb Space Telescope's Mid-Infrared Instrument (MIRI) reported in 2024 the detection of carbon dioxide (CO2) in the atmosphere of the exoplanet WASP-39b, a hot Saturn orbiting a G-type star roughly 700 light-years from Earth. The team measured a CO2 abundance of approximately 1,000 parts per million by volume at the day-side terminator region, based on transit spectroscopy data from two JWST eclipses observed in 2022 and 2023. The detection is statistically significant at the 26-sigma level. The authors noted that the measured abundance is consistent with predictions from models that assume WASP-39b formed beyond the snow line and migrated inward, but they cautioned that the result represents a single terminator measurement and should not be generalized to the planet's full atmosphere."

Leia o texto abaixo e escreva um resumo de exatamente duas frases em português.
Restrição 1: A primeira frase deve indicar a data exata (dia, mês e ano) da Batalha de Aljubarrota e os nomes dos dois líderes das forças portuguesas.
Restrição 2: A segunda frase deve descrever as defesas físicas terrestres usadas pelo exército português e o resultado político dessa vitória para Portugal.
Restrição 3: Não mencione a Batalha dos Atoleiros, a Batalha de Valverde, nem os anos 1383 ou 1384.

Texto:
Durante a crise sucessória de 1383-1385, ocorreram importantes confrontos militares. Na Batalha dos Atoleiros, em 1384, D. Nuno Álvares Pereira usou a tática do quadrado contra Castela. Em 14 de agosto de 1385, ocorreu a célebre Batalha de Aljubarrota, onde o exército português, sob a liderança de D. João I e do condestável D. Nuno Álvares Pereira, derrotou as tropas de D. Juan I de Castela. As forças portuguesas usaram fortificações terrestres com fossos e valas para anular a cavalaria castelhana, e a vitória consolidou a Dinastia de Avis e garantiu a independência de Portugal. Mais tarde, em outubro de 1385, a Batalha de Valverde consolidou a posição militar portuguesa na fronteira.

Lee el siguiente artículo y produce un resumen de una sola oración de máximo 35 palabras que identifique el hallazgo principal, la población estudiada y el resultado numérico reportado. No incluyas contexto histórico, metodología ni implicaciones.

Artículo:
"Un ensayo clínico aleatorizado publicado en 2023 en la revista The Lancet Diabetes & Endocrinology evaluó el efecto de un programa de 12 meses de ejercicio aeróbico supervisado sobre 320 adultos con diabetes tipo 2 y sobrepeso (IMC entre 28 y 35) en cuatro hospitales públicos de España. Los participantes fueron asignados al azar a un grupo de ejercicio aeróbico supervisado tres veces por semana (n=160) o a un grupo de atención habitual con recomendaciones escritas (n=160). La variable principal fue el cambio en la hemoglobina glicosilada (HbA1c) a los 12 meses. El grupo de ejercicio redujo la HbA1c en 0.8 puntos porcentuales (de 8.2% a 7.4%), mientras que el grupo de atención habitual la redujo en 0.2 puntos (de 8.1% a 7.9%). La diferencia intergrupal de 0.6 puntos fue estadísticamente significativa (p=0.003). Los autores concluyeron que el ejercicio aeróbico supervisado produjo mejoras clínicamente relevantes en el control glucémico de adultos españoles con diabetes tipo 2, aunque señalaron que el efecto podría atenuarse tras finalizar la intervención supervisada."

Read the following historical text and provide a summary according to the constraints below.

Source Text:
'In 1901, the Discovery Expedition set sail, led by Robert Falcon Scott with Ernest Shackleton as third lieutenant. They reached the Ross Ice Shelf in early 1902. In November 1902, Scott, Shackleton, and Edward Wilson marched south, reaching a record southern latitude of 82°17'S on December 30, 1902. Shackleton fell ill during the return journey and was sent home on the relief ship Morning in March 1903. Later, Shackleton led the Nimrod Expedition in 1907. He established a base at Cape Royds in February 1908. In January 1909, Shackleton and three companions reached a new Furthest South of 88°23'S. Meanwhile, Scott was planning his next venture, the Terra Nova Expedition, which departed in 1910. Scott reached the South Pole on January 17, 1912, only to find Roald Amundsen had beaten him. Scott and his final companions perished on the return journey in March 1912. Shackleton's Imperial Trans-Antarctic Expedition commenced in 1914 on the Endurance. The ship became trapped in ice in January 1915 and sank in November 1915. Shackleton led his men to Elephant Island by April 1916. He then made a 800-mile open-boat journey in the James Caird, arriving at South Georgia in May 1916, eventually rescuing all his men by August 1916. Shackleton died of a heart attack in January 1922 at South Georgia during the Quest Expedition.'

Constraints:
1. Summarize only the Antarctic expeditions led by Ernest Shackleton.
2. Do not include any details about the Discovery Expedition or Robert Falcon Scott's Terra Nova Expedition.
3. The summary must consist of exactly three sentences.
4. The summary must describe Shackleton's expeditions in chronological order.
5. The years 1907, 1914, and 1922 must be explicitly mentioned in the summary.

Read the following article and produce a single-sentence summary of at most 35 words that captures the main finding, the specific population studied, and the reported effect size. Do not include any background, methodology, or caveats.

Article:
A randomized controlled trial published in 2023 enrolled 412 adults aged 60 and older with mild cognitive impairment across four memory clinics in the Netherlands. Participants were randomly assigned to a 12-week aerobic exercise program (n=206) or a stretching-and-toning control group (n=206). The primary outcome was change in the Alzheimer's Disease Assessment Scale-Cognitive Subscale (ADAS-Cog) score at 24 weeks. The exercise group showed a mean improvement of 1.8 points (95% CI 0.9 to 2.7) compared with the control group, a statistically significant difference (p=0.0003). Secondary analyses suggested larger benefits among participants with baseline ADAS-Cog scores above the median. The authors concluded that structured aerobic exercise produces modest but measurable cognitive gains in older adults with mild cognitive impairment, though they noted that longer-term outcomes and translation to dementia incidence remain unstudied.

Read the following historical text about the noble gases and provide a summary according to the constraints below.

Source Text:
'In 1785, Henry Cavendish found that air contains a small fraction of unreactive gas, though he could not identify it. In 1868, Pierre Janssen and Joseph Norman Lockyer detected a new element in the solar spectrum during a solar eclipse, naming it helium. It was not until 1894 that Lord Rayleigh and William Ramsay discovered argon by removing oxygen, nitrogen, carbon dioxide, and water from a sample of clean air. Ramsay also isolated helium on Earth in 1905 by treating cleveite with acid, though others had noted it earlier. In 1898, Ramsay and Morris Travers discovered krypton, neon, and xenon by liquefying air and separating it into fractions. Radon was first identified in 1899 by Ernest Rutherford and Robert Owens as a radioactive gas emitted by thorium, but Friedrich Ernst Dorn is often credited with discovering it in 1900 as a radium decay product. Finally, in 1962, Neil Bartlett prepared the first noble gas compound, xenon hexafluoroplatinate, proving that these gases were not entirely inert.'

Constraints:
1. Summarize only the discoveries of argon, krypton, neon, and xenon.
2. Do not mention helium, radon, or the work of Henry Cavendish or Neil Bartlett.
3. The summary must be written in exactly two sentences.
4. The summary must mention the years 1894 and 1898.
5. The summary must name the scientists Lord Rayleigh, William Ramsay, and Morris Travers.

Read the following article and produce a single-sentence summary of at most 30 words that states the central claim, the type of evidence supporting it, and the most important counterargument the article acknowledges. Do not include background, definitions, or recommendations.

Article:
A 2024 policy brief from a Washington think tank argues that the United States should adopt a federal value-added tax (VAT) of 5 percent to help fund entitlement programs and reduce deficits. The brief cites cross-national evidence from the 38 OECD countries, noting that the 34 OECD members with a VAT collect on average about 20 percent of total tax revenue from the tax, compared with roughly 0 percent in the United States. The authors claim that a 5 percent U.S. VAT could raise approximately $250 billion annually by year ten. The brief acknowledges the principal counterargument that a VAT is regressive, disproportionately burdening lower-income households, but contends that targeted rebates can offset roughly 80 percent of the regressivity based on Canadian and British rebate-program evaluations. Critics outside the brief have also questioned the $250 billion projection, citing assumptions about compliance and evasion rates.

Read the following article and produce a single-sentence summary of at most 35 words that identifies the main event, the two opposing sides, and the specific outcome. Do not include historical background, casualty figures, or diplomatic context beyond what is needed to identify the event.

Article:
On 14 February 2023, the International Court of Justice (ICJ) issued its judgment in the case of The Gambia v. Myanmar, concerning the application of the Convention on the Prevention and Punishment of the Crime of Genocide. The Gambia argued that Myanmar had violated its obligations under the Genocide Convention in its treatment of the Rohingya minority, particularly following the 2017 military operations in Rakhine State. Myanmar denied the allegations and contested the Court's jurisdiction. In a unanimous ruling, the ICJ rejected Myanmar's preliminary objections and affirmed that the case could proceed on the merits, while also ordering Myanmar to take all measures within its power to prevent genocidal acts against the Rohingya and to preserve evidence. The Court did not make a final determination on whether genocide had occurred, reserving that question for a later phase of the proceedings.

Read the following text and provide a summary according to the constraints below.

Source Text:
'The classification of living organisms has evolved significantly. In 1735, Carl Linnaeus published Systema Naturae, dividing nature into three kingdoms: animal, vegetable, and mineral. In 1866, Ernst Haeckel proposed a three-kingdom system, introducing Protista for single-celled organisms alongside Plantae and Animalia. Édouard Chatton introduced the terms prokaryote and eukaryote in 1925 to distinguish organisms without and with cell nuclei. In 1938, Herbert Copeland proposed a four-kingdom system, elevating Monera to a kingdom for prokaryotes. Robert Whittaker proposed the famous five-kingdom system in 1969, adding Fungi as a separate kingdom. Finally, in 1977, Carl Woese and George Fox introduced the three-domain system (Archaea, Bacteria, and Eucarya) based on ribosomal RNA analysis, which redefined the tree of life.'

Constraints:
1. Summarize only the classification systems proposed between 1900 and 1970.
2. Do not mention Carl Linnaeus, Ernst Haeckel, or Carl Woese.
3. The summary must consist of exactly two sentences.
4. The summary must name the kingdoms or domains introduced in the systems being summarized.
5. The years 1938 and 1969 must be explicitly mentioned.

Read the following article and produce a single-sentence summary of at most 30 words that states the main finding, the methodology used, and the most important limitation the authors acknowledge. Do not include policy implications or future research suggestions.

Article:
A 2024 study in a peer-reviewed climate journal used a combination of satellite remote-sensing data and ground-based temperature records from 1980 to 2022 to estimate trends in urban heat island intensity across 142 major cities worldwide. The authors reported an average intensification of 0.31 degrees Celsius per decade over the study period, with the strongest trends observed in mid-latitude cities that had undergone rapid expansion of impervious surface area. The methodology combined MODIS land-surface temperature retrievals with homogenized station data, adjusted for sensor drift and cloud contamination. The authors explicitly acknowledged that their estimates may underestimate heat island intensity in cities with sparse ground-station coverage, particularly in parts of sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia, and that the satellite-derived land-surface temperatures do not directly correspond to the air temperatures experienced by residents.

'A história da exploração espacial portuguesa e suas parcerias internacionais revela marcos importantes. Em 1993, o primeiro satélite português, PoSAT-1, foi lançado ao espaço a partir de Kourou, na Guiana Francesa, a bordo de um foguete Ariane 4. O projeto foi liderado por Fernando Carvalho Rodrigues. Em 2000, Portugal tornou-se oficialmente membro da Agência Espacial Europeia (ESA). Em 2019, foi criada a agência espacial nacional, Portugal Space, com sede em Ponta Delgada. Em setembro de 2023, o projeto 'Aeros MH-1', um nano-satélite de 4,5 kg desenvolvido por um consórcio português liderado pela CEiiA, foi concluído e enviado para os Estados Unidos, sendo posteriormente lançado em março de 2024 a bordo de um foguete Falcon 9 da SpaceX. Outro projeto importante, o 'Infante', planejado para ser um microssatélite de observação da Terra, foi iniciado em 2018 sob a coordenação da Tekever, mas sofreu reestruturações significativas ao longo dos anos.'

Restrições:

2. Não mencione a criação da Portugal Space, a adesão à ESA ou o projeto Infante.

4. O resumo deve mencionar os anos de 1993 e 2023.

Artículo:
Un estudio publicado en 2024 por un equipo de ecólogos marinos de la Universidad de Barcelona analizó la evolución de las poblaciones del coralígeno mediterráneo en 27 reservas marinas分布于 el mar Mediterráneo occidental y central entre 2005 y 2022. El equipo combinó censos visuales submarinos con modelos de ocupación que incorporaban variables de temperatura superficial del mar y de presión pesquera. Los autores reportaron una reducción promedio del 18 por ciento en la cobertura de coralígeno en las reservas estudiadas, con las disminuciones más pronunciadas en zonas donde la temperatura superficial del mar superó en más de 1 grado Celsius la media histórica del período 1985-2004. El estudio concluyó que el calentamiento marino, y no la presión pesquera, fue el principal factor correlacionado con la pérdida de cobertura durante el período analizado.

Devuelve el resultado como una lista de elementos, donde cada elemento es un objeto JSON con tres claves: "entity_text" (la cadena exacta del texto), "entity_type" (uno de: PERSONA, ORG, LUGAR, FECHA, DINERO), y "offset" (el índice del primer carácter de la cadena, basado en 0).

Texto:
"El 12 de octubre de 2022, la Dra. María Fernández del Consejo Superior de Investigaciones Científicas anunció una beca de 2.500.000 euros concedida por la Unión Europea para investigar el cambio climático en la Sierra de Guadarrama. El equipo, que incluye colaboradores de la Universidad Autónoma de Madrid y del Instituto Geológico y Minero de España, viajará desde Madrid el 5 de marzo de 2023 y permanecerá seis meses en la sierra. Fernández señaló que estudios similares realizados en 2019 cerca del Parque Nacional de Doñana habían mostrado resultados prometedores, y espera publicar los hallazgos antes de diciembre de 2024."

Definiciones de tipos de entidad:
- PERSONA: seres humanos individuales (los títulos como Dra., Sr., Sra. no forman parte de la entidad).
- ORG: organizaciones, empresas, instituciones, agencias.
- LUGAR: ubicaciones geográficas con nombre, incluyendo ciudades, comunidades, regiones, países y accidentes geográficos (sierras, parques nacionales).
- FECHA: fechas del calendario o años/meses escritos en forma de fecha.
- DINERO: cantidades monetarias con un símbolo de moneda o la palabra "euros/millones" asociada a una cantidad.

Formato de salida: una lista JSON. Ejemplo: [{"entity_text": "Empresa X", "entity_type": "ORG", "offset": 10}]

Passage:
"On March 14, 1879, Albert Einstein was born in Ulm, a city in the Kingdom of Württemberg in the German Empire. His father, Hermann Einstein, ran a small electrochemical factory with his brother Jakob Einstein. In 1894, the family moved to Milan after the Einstein brothers lost a contract with AEG, the Allgemeine Elektricitäts-Gesellschaft. After completing his education at the Luitpold Gymnasium in Munich, young Albert renounced his German citizenship in 1896 and enrolled at the Swiss Federal Polytechnic in Zurich. He graduated in 1900 and took Swiss citizenship in 1901. From 1902 to 1909 he worked at the Bern patent office, evaluating electromagnetic devices. In 1905, while still at the patent office, he published four groundbreaking papers in Annalen der Physik. In 1921 he received the Nobel Prize in Physics, awarded by the Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences, and the prize money of about 121,572 Swedish kronor was deposited in a Swiss bank account. During the 1930s, after the rise of the Nazi Party, he emigrated to the United States and joined the Institute for Advanced Study in Princeton, New Jersey, on October 17, 1933. He lived at 112 Mercer Street until his death on April 18, 1955, at Princeton Hospital."

In contrast, Imatinib, another small molecule, was approved by the FDA in 2001 to inhibit the BCR-ABL protein. For cancer treatment, Pembrolizumab is a highly effective monoclonal antibody approved in 2014 that targets the PD-1 protein. To combat viral infections, Remdesivir (approved in 2020) is a small molecule targeting the viral RdRp protein of SARS-CoV-2. Paxlovid contains Nirmatrelvir (a small molecule approved in 2021) which targets the viral Mpro protein. Additionally, the small molecule drug Tafamidis was approved in 2019 and binds to the TTR protein in humans. Lastly, Teplizumab is a monoclonal antibody approved in 2022 that targets the CD3 protein on T cells.

Passage:
"Charles Dickens published 'A Tale of Two Cities' in 1859, a novel set in London and Paris during the French Revolution. The book was serialized in 1859 in his own magazine, 'All the Year Round', which Dickens had founded in 1859 with his sub-editor William Henry Wills. Dickens had previously serialized 'Oliver Twist' between 1837 and 1839 in 'Bentley's Miscellany', edited by Richard Bentley. In 1843, Dickens wrote 'A Christmas Carol' while living in Turin, and the following year he began 'The Chimes' during a stay in Genoa. His later collaboration with Wilkie Collins produced 'The Lazy Tour of Two Idle Apprentices' in 1857. Dickens toured the United States in 1842, reading from 'The Pickwick Papers' and meeting writers such as Washington Irving and Edgar Allan Poe. He died on June 9, 1870, at Gad's Hill Place in Kent, and was buried in Poets' Corner at Westminster Abbey."

The spacecraft entered lunar orbit on July 19, 1969, and the Lunar Module Eagle landed in the Sea of Tranquility on July 20, 1969. Armstrong stepped onto the lunar surface at 10:56 PM Eastern time, followed by Aldrin, while Collins remained in the Command Module Columbia. The mission was a key event of the Space Race between the United States and the Soviet Union. Earlier, the Soviet Luna 2 probe had crashed into the Moon in 1959, and Yuri Gagarin had become the first human in space on April 12, 1961, during the Vostok 1 mission. The Apollo program was managed by NASA, headquartered in Washington, D.C., and the astronauts returned safely to Earth on July 24, 1969, splashing down in the Pacific Ocean near Hawaii. The crew was quarantined at the Lunar Receiving Laboratory in Houston, Texas, before President Richard Nixon welcomed them aboard the USS Hornet on July 25, 1969."

Por outro lado, a Guiana Francesa continua sendo um território ultramarino da França e nunca se tornou um país independente, embora Léon Damas seja uma figura histórica importante lá. Texto:
"Gabriel García Márquez nació el 6 de marzo de 1927 en Aracataca, Colombia. Creció en la casa de sus abuelos, el coronel Nicolás Ricardo Márquez Mejía y Tranquilina Iguarán Cotes, en una región cercana al río Magdalena. Estudió derecho en la Universidad Nacional de Colombia en Bogotá y luego en la Universidad de Cartagena. En 1955 publicó 'La hojarasca' con la editorial Aguirre Editor en Medellín, y en 1967 publicó 'Cien años de soledad' con la Editorial Sudamericana en Buenos Aires, Argentina. La novela fue traducida a más de treinta idiomas y consolidó el realismo mágico como movimiento literario. En 1982 recibió el Premio Nobel de Literatura en Estocolmo, Suecia, otorgado por la Academia Sueca, y el monto del premio, alrededor de 1.600.000 coronas suecas, fue depositado en una cuenta bancaria. García Márquez vivió en México D.F. desde 1961, donde fundó el Instituto Cinematográfico y Cultural de América Latina junto con otros cineastas. Murió el 17 de abril de 2014 en la Ciudad de México."

Texto:
"El pasado 12 de octubre de 2023, durante la ceremonia celebrada en el Palacio de la Moncloa en Madrid, la presidenta del Gobierno de España, Isabel Díaz Ayuso —acompañada por el ministro de Economía, Carlos Cuerpo, y por la presidenta de la Comunidad de Madrid, Enrique Ossorio— firmó junto al presidente de la Confederación Española de Organizaciones Empresariales (CEOE), Antonio Garamendi, un nuevo convenio de colaboración económica entre el Reino de España y la República Argentina, representado por su embajador en España, Ricardo Alfonsín. El acuerdo, valorado en 3.500 millones de euros (aproximadamente un 4,2 % del PIB argentino previsto para 2024), contempla la creación de 12.000 puestos de trabajo directos en sectores como el automotriz, el farmacéutico y el agroalimentario, y la exportación de productos españoles como el aceite de oliva, el vino de Rioja y los embutidos de la marca Joselito. La lengua oficial del tratado será el español, aunque se incluirán anexos en catalán, euskera, gallego y portugués. El evento, bautizado como 'Cumbre Iberoamericana de Madrid 2023', contó con la presencia de destacados economistas como el Nobel de Economía Paul Krugman, el profesor de Harvard Dani Rodrik y la catedrática de la Universidad de Salamanca Carmen Reinhart. Durante su intervención, Krugman citó su último libro, 'El retorno de la economía de los grandes ciclos', publicado por la editorial Planeta en septiembre de 2023. La reunión concluyó con la lectura de un comunicado conjunto redactado en lengua castellana y firmado por ambos mandatarios, en el que se anunció la creación del 'Fondo Hispano-Argentino de Inversión Estratégica', dotado con 750 millones de dólares estadounidenses y gestionado por el Banco Santander junto con el Banco de la Nación Argentina. Entre los asistentes también se encontraba el futbolista Lionel Messi, natural de Rosario, quien actuó en calidad de Embajador de Buena Voluntad de la UNESCO, y la cantante Rosalía, nacida en Barcelona, que interpretó el himno 'Marcha Real' en lengua castellana y cerró el acto con una versión en catalán de la 'Sardana' tradicional."

The following Python function is supposed to flatten one level of nesting from a list of iterables and return a new list. It contains a bug. Fix the bug so that the function behaves exactly as specified.

```python
from collections.abc import Iterable

def flatten_one_level(nested):
    result = []
    for item in nested:
        if isinstance(item, Iterable):
            for sub in item:
                result.append(sub)
        else:
            result.append(item)
    return result
```

Behavioral requirement:
- For each element in the input that is a list or tuple, expand it into its individual elements in the output.
- Elements that are not lists or tuples (including strings, ints, dicts, sets) must be appended as-is.
- Strings must NOT be expanded character-by-character.
- The original nesting depth of expanded elements must be preserved (no further flattening).
- An empty input list must return an empty list.

Return only the corrected Python function.

The following Python function is intended to find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters. It contains a bug where the `start` pointer can incorrectly move backwards when a repeating character is encountered outside the current sliding window.

```python
def longest_unique_substring(s: str) -> int:
    char_map = {}
    max_len = 0
    start = 0
    for end in range(len(s)):
        if s[end] in char_map:
            start = char_map[s[end]] + 1
        char_map[s[end]] = end
        max_len = max(max_len, end - start + 1)
    return max_len
```

Fix this bug so that `start` only moves forward (or stays the same) to ensure the sliding window remains valid. Return only the corrected Python code.

```python
        raise ValueError("n must be non-negative")
    a, b = 0, 1

    return a
```

Behavioral requirement:

- F(1) must return 1.

- F(10) must return 55.

You are debugging an A* pathfinding algorithm on a grid. The algorithm finds the shortest path between a start and end point, but with an additional constraint: changing direction (turning) incurs a penalty cost `turn_cost`.

Here is the current implementation:

```python
import heapq

def shortest_path_with_turns(grid, start, end, turn_cost):
    rows, cols = len(grid), len(grid[0])
    
    # Priority queue stores: (estimated_total_cost, current_cost, r, c, direction)
    pq = []
    for d in range(4):
        heapq.heappush(pq, (0, 0, start[0], start[1], d))
        
    visited = set()
    directions = [(-1, 0), (0, 1), (1, 0), (0, -1)]  # 0: Up, 1: Right, 2: Down, 3: Left
    
    while pq:
        _, cost, r, c, d = heapq.heappop(pq)
        
        if (r, c) == end:
            return cost
            
        if (r, c) in visited:
            continue
        visited.add((r, c))
        
        for next_d in range(4):
            dr, dc = directions[next_d]
            nr, nc = r + dr, c + dc
            
            if 0 <= nr < rows and 0 <= nc < cols and grid[nr][nc] == 0:
                move_cost = 1
                if next_d != d:
                    move_cost += turn_cost
                
                next_cost = cost + move_cost
                h = abs(nr - end[0]) + abs(nc - end[1])
                heapq.heappush(pq, (next_cost + h, next_cost, nr, nc, next_d))
                
    return -1
```

### The Problem
The implementation fails to find the optimal path in some grids because the `visited` set only tracks coordinates `(r, c)`. Once a cell is visited from one direction, it is marked as visited. However, entering that same cell from a different direction might allow for a much cheaper path later (due to avoiding turning penalties). Thus, the actual shortest path is pruned prematurely.

### Requirements
Modify `shortest_path_with_turns` to:
1. Track visited states using both coordinates and direction `(r, c, d)` instead of just `(r, c)` to prevent premature path pruning.
2. Correctly compute step costs: if the new direction `next_d` is different from `d`, add `turn_cost` to the cost.
3. Return the minimum cost to reach `end` from `start`, or `-1` if no path is found.

Return only the corrected implementation.

Você está depurando uma implementação em Python de um Buffer Circular (Ring Buffer) de tamanho fixo. O objetivo é permitir a inserção contínua de elementos. Quando o buffer atinge sua capacidade máxima, novas inserções devem sobrescrever o elemento mais antigo (comportamento FIFO).

Aqui está a implementação atual com falhas:

```python
class RingBuffer:
    def __init__(self, capacity: int):
        self.capacity = capacity
        self.buffer = [None] * capacity
        self.head = 0
        self.tail = 0
        self.size = 0

    def append(self, item):
        self.buffer[self.tail] = item
        self.tail = (self.tail + 1) % self.capacity
        
        if self.size < self.capacity:
            self.size += 1
        else:
            # Bug: Quando o buffer está cheio, sobrescrevemos o elemento no índice tail,
            # que coincide com head. Portanto, head deve avançar para o próximo elemento mais antigo.
            pass

    def pop(self):
        if self.size == 0:
            return None
        
        item = self.buffer[self.head]
        self.buffer[self.head] = None
        self.head = (self.head + 1) % self.capacity
        self.size -= 1
        return item
```

### O Problema
Quando o buffer fica cheio (ou seja, `size == capacity`) e um novo item é adicionado via `append`, o elemento mais antigo (atualmente apontado por `head`) é sobrescrito. No entanto, `head` não é atualizado. Como resultado, chamadas subsequentes a `pop` retornam incorretamente o item recém-inserido em vez do próximo item mais antigo.

### Requisitos
Modifique a classe `RingBuffer` para:
1. Atualizar o ponteiro `head` corretamente quando um item for inserido em um buffer que já está cheio.
2. Garantir que `pop` retorne os elementos na ordem correta de inserção (do mais antigo para o mais recente) e atualize o `size` de forma apropriada.

Retorne apenas a implementação corrigida da classe `RingBuffer`.

The following Python class implements a thread-safe token bucket rate limiter. However, it contains a bug: under high-frequency requests, the bucket fails to refill correctly and leads to starvation, even if the average request rate is well below the refill rate.

```python
import time
from threading import Lock

class TokenBucket:
    def __init__(self, capacity: int, refill_rate: int):
        self.capacity = capacity
        self.refill_rate = refill_rate # tokens per second
        self.tokens = capacity
        self.last_refill = time.time()
        self.lock = Lock()

    def consume(self, amount: int) -> bool:
        with self.lock:
            now = time.time()
            elapsed = now - self.last_refill
            refill = elapsed * self.refill_rate
            if refill >= 1:
                self.tokens = min(self.capacity, self.tokens + int(refill))
            self.last_refill = now
            
            if self.tokens >= amount:
                self.tokens -= amount
                return True
            return False
```

Identify the bug and provide the corrected version of the `consume` method. The fix must ensure that the fractional elapsed time is preserved, so that high-frequency calls do not reset the refill timer and starve the bucket.

A classe Python a seguir implementa um limitador de taxa por janela deslizante (`SlidingWindowRateLimiter`) thread-safe. No entanto, ela contém um erro na remoção de requisições expiradas da fila que faz com que requisições recentes e válidas sejam descartadas incorretamente, permitindo comportamento inesperado e falha no controle do limite.

```python
import time
from collections import deque
from threading import Lock

class SlidingWindowRateLimiter:
    def __init__(self, max_requests: int, window_size: float):
        self.max_requests = max_requests
        self.window_size = window_size
        self.requests = {}
        self.lock = Lock()

    def allow_request(self, client_id: str) -> bool:
        with self.lock:
            now = time.time()
            if client_id not in self.requests:
                self.requests[client_id] = deque()
            
            client_queue = self.requests[client_id]
            while client_queue and client_queue[0] < now - self.window_size:
                client_queue.pop()
                
            if len(client_queue) < self.max_requests:
                client_queue.append(now)
                return True
```

### Buggy Code:
```python

from collections import defaultdict, deque

def sortItems(n: int, m: int, group: List[int], beforeItems: List[List[int]]) -> List[int]:
    # Group-to-items mapping
    group_to_items = defaultdict(list)
    for i in range(n):
        group_to_items[group[i]].append(i)
        
    # Build graphs
    group_graph = defaultdict(list)
    group_in_degree = defaultdict(int)
    
    item_graph = defaultdict(list)
    item_in_degree = [0] * n
    
    for i in range(n):
        to_group = group[i]
        for pr in beforeItems[i]:
            from_group = group[pr]
            if from_group != to_group:
                group_graph[from_group].append(to_group)
                group_in_degree[to_group] += 1
            item_graph[pr].append(i)
            item_in_degree[i] += 1
            
    # Helper topological sort
    def topo_sort(nodes, graph, in_degree):
        queue = deque([node for node in nodes if in_degree[node] == 0])
        res = []
        while queue:
            curr = queue.popleft()
            res.append(curr)
            for neighbor in graph[curr]:
                in_degree[neighbor] -= 1
                if in_degree[neighbor] == 0:
                    queue.append(neighbor)
        return res if len(res) == len(nodes) else []

    # Topological sort for groups
    unique_groups = list(group_to_items.keys())
    sorted_groups = topo_sort(unique_groups, group_graph, group_in_degree)
    if not sorted_groups and unique_groups:
        return []
        
    # Topological sort for items globally
    all_items = list(range(n))
    sorted_items = topo_sort(all_items, item_graph, item_in_degree)
    if not sorted_items:
        return []
        
    # Group the sorted items

    for item in sorted_items:

    ans = []
    for g in sorted_groups:
```

The following Python function uses Dijkstra's algorithm to find the fastest path between two nodes in a graph with battery constraints. The battery level is capped at `max_battery` and cannot go below 0.

However, the function contains a bug where it prematurely prunes valid paths due to incorrect state tracking.

```python
import heapq

def find_fastest_path(graph, start, end, initial_battery, max_battery):
    """
    Finds the fastest path in a directed graph from start to end node.
    Each edge in the graph is represented as (neighbor, battery_cost, time_cost).
    The battery charge must remain between 0 and max_battery (inclusive) at all times.
    If the battery level after traversing an edge exceeds max_battery, it is capped at max_battery.
    If the battery level drops below 0, the path is invalid.
    Returns the minimum time to reach the end node, or -1 if it is unreachable.
    """
    pq = [(0, start, initial_battery)]
    visited = set()
    while pq:
        time, node, battery = heapq.heappop(pq)
        if node == end:
            return time
        if node in visited:
            continue
        visited.add(node)
        for neighbor, cost, t in graph.get(node, []):
            next_battery = battery - cost
            if next_battery > max_battery:
                next_battery = max_battery
            if next_battery >= 0:
                heapq.heappush(pq, (time + t, neighbor, next_battery))
    return -1
```

Fix the function so it correctly tracks visited states without losing paths that might have more time but higher battery levels (which may be needed to complete the journey). Your output must contain only the corrected Python code.

The following Python class implements a Sliding Window Counter Rate Limiter.
It has two bugs:
1. It does not clean up older windows from client history, causing memory leaks over time.
2. In estimating the previous window's request count, it fetches the most recent window in history even if that window is much older than the immediately preceding window (which should contribute 0 count).

```python
class SlidingWindowRateLimiter:
    def __init__(self, max_requests, window_size=60):
        self.max_requests = max_requests
        self.window_size = window_size
        self.client_data = {}  # client_id -> {window_start -> count}

    def allow_request(self, client_id, current_time):
        if client_id not in self.client_data:
            self.client_data[client_id] = {}
        
        history = self.client_data[client_id]
        current_window = int(current_time // self.window_size) * self.window_size
        prev_window = current_window - self.window_size

        sorted_windows = sorted(history.keys())
        prev_count = 0
        if sorted_windows:
            last_window = None
            for w in sorted_windows:
                if w < current_window:
                    last_window = w
            if last_window is not None:
                prev_count = history[last_window]

        current_count = history.get(current_window, 0)
        elapsed = current_time - current_window
        weight = (self.window_size - elapsed) / self.window_size
        estimated = prev_count * weight + current_count

        if estimated < self.max_requests:
            history[current_window] = current_count + 1
            return True
        return False
```

Fix the code to:
1. Safely remove any window starts that are older than `prev_window` from the client's history.
2. Ensure that `prev_count` only uses the count from the immediately preceding window (`prev_window`), using 0 if it does not exist.
Your output must contain only the corrected Python code.

A seguinte função Python ordena tarefas com base em suas dependências (ordenação topológica).
Ela possui dois bugs:
1. Não valida se as tarefas mencionadas em `dependencias` existem na lista `tarefas`, o que pode causar comportamento incorreto ou falhar silenciosamente em vez de lançar um `ValueError`.
2. Não detecta ciclos nas dependências. Caso haja um ciclo, ela retorna apenas as tarefas fora do ciclo em vez de lançar um `ValueError`.

```python
import heapq

def agendar_tarefas(tarefas, dependencias):
    """
    Ordena as tarefas de acordo com as dependências fornecidas.
    Se houver um ciclo ou se as dependências referenciarem tarefas que não estão na lista 'tarefas',
    deve lançar um ValueError.
    Em caso de múltiplas ordens válidas, retorna a ordem lexicograficamente menor.
    """
    adj = {t: set() for t in tarefas}
    grau_entrada = {t: 0 for t in tarefas}
    
    for u, v in dependencias:
        if u not in adj or v not in adj:
            if u not in adj:
                continue
        adj[u].add(v)
        grau_entrada[v] += 1
        
    fila = [t for t in tarefas if grau_entrada[t] == 0]
    heapq.heapify(fila)
    resultado = []
    
    while fila:
        u = heapq.heappop(fila)
        resultado.append(u)
        for v in adj[u]:
            grau_entrada[v] -= 1
            if grau_entrada[v] == 0:
                heapq.heappush(fila, v)
                
    return resultado
```

Corrija a função de forma que:
1. Lance `ValueError` se alguma dependência contiver tarefas que não constam na lista `tarefas`.
2. Lance `ValueError` se houver um ciclo (quando nem todas as tarefas puderem ser ordenadas).
3. Mantenha a ordenação lexicográfica para resolver múltiplos caminhos válidos.

Se te proporciona la siguiente función en Python que intenta calcular el número de subconjuntos no vacíos de un conjunto cuyo producto de elementos sea divisible por un entero dado `k`. La función debe devolver el resultado módulo `10^9 + 7`. Sin embargo, contiene errores. Corrige la función para que cumpla exactamente con la especificación.

```python
def count_divisible_subsets(arr, k):
    MOD = 10**9 + 7
    n = len(arr)
    # dp[i] = número de subconjuntos con producto congruente con i módulo k
    dp = [0] * k
    dp[1 % k] = 1  # subconjunto vacío con producto 1

    for x in arr:
        new_dp = dp[:]
        for r in range(k):
            if dp[r]:
                new_r = (r * x) % k
                new_dp[new_r] = (new_dp[new_r] + dp[r]) % MOD
        dp = new_dp

    return dp[0]
```

- La complejidad debe ser `O(n * k)` en tiempo y `O(k)` en espacio adicional (sin contar la entrada).

```python
        self.not_full = threading.Condition(self.lock)
        self.not_empty = threading.Condition(self.lock)

    def enqueue(self, item):
        with self.lock:
            if len(self.queue) >= self.capacity:
                self.not_full.wait()
            self.queue.append(item)
            self.not_empty.notify()

    def dequeue(self):
```

The following Python function is supposed to merge two sorted lists of integers (ascending, possibly with duplicates) into a single sorted list (ascending) without using any built-in sort. It must preserve all duplicates. It must return a new list and must not mutate either input list. It must correctly handle empty inputs (returning a copy of the non-empty one, or an empty list if both are empty).

```python
def merge_sorted(a, b):
    i = 0
    j = 0
    out = []
    while i < len(a) and j < len(b):
        if a[i] <= b[j]:
            out.append(a[i])
            i += 1
        else:
            out.append(b[j])
            j += 1
    out.extend(a[i:])
    out.extend(b[j:])
    return out
```

The following Python implementation of an LRU Cache uses a hash map and a doubly linked list. The cache should evict the least recently used item when the capacity is exceeded. However, there is a bug in the `put` method: when evicting the least recently used node from the doubly linked list, the key is never deleted from the `self.cache` dictionary. Fix the `put` method to ensure the evicted key is properly deleted from the dictionary.

Buggy code:
```python
class Node:
    def __init__(self, key=0, value=0):
        self.key = key
        self.value = value
        self.prev = None
        self.next = None

class LRUCache:
    def __init__(self, capacity: int):
        self.capacity = capacity
        self.cache = {}
        self.head = Node()
        self.tail = Node()
        self.head.next = self.tail
        self.tail.prev = self.head

    def _remove(self, node):
        prev = node.prev
        nxt = node.next
        prev.next = nxt
        nxt.prev = prev

    def _add(self, node):
        node.prev = self.tail.prev
        node.next = self.tail
        self.tail.prev.next = node
        self.tail.prev = node

    def get(self, key: int) -> int:
        if key in self.cache:
            node = self.cache[key]
            self._remove(node)
            self._add(node)
            return node.value
        return -1

    def put(self, key: int, value: int) -> None:
        if key in self.cache:
            self._remove(self.cache[key])
        node = Node(key, value)
        self.cache[key] = node
        self._add(node)
        if len(self.cache) > self.capacity:
            lru = self.head.next
            self._remove(lru)
```

Return only the corrected Python code containing both classes inside a ```python ``` block.

The following Python function is supposed to compute the n-th Fibonacci number using 0-indexing where fib(0) = 0 and fib(1) = 1. It must return an int. It must correctly handle n = 0 and n = 1. It must raise a ValueError when n is a negative integer. It must not use recursion (to avoid stack overflow for large n).

```python
def fib(n: int) -> int:
    if n < 0:
        raise ValueError("n must be non-negative")
    if n == 0:
        return 0
    if n == 1:
        return 1
    a = 0
    b = 1
    for _ in range(n - 1):
        c = a + b
        a = b
        b = c
    return a
```

Corrija a seguinte função em Python `menor_caminho` que encontra o comprimento do caminho mais curto em uma matriz binária (onde 0 é livre e 1 é obstáculo) usando a busca em largura (BFS). A função contém um bug de eficiência e correção: ela marca os nós como visitados apenas quando são removidos da fila (`popleft`), em vez de quando são inseridos. Isso permite que nós idênticos sejam adicionados à fila múltiplas vezes se forem alcançados por caminhos diferentes antes de serem processados.

Código com bug:
```python
from collections import deque

def menor_caminho(grid, inicio, fim):
    linhas, colunas = len(grid), len(grid[0])
    fila = deque([(inicio[0], inicio[1], 0)])
    visitados = set()
    
    while fila:
        r, c, dist = fila.popleft()
        if (r, c) == fim:
            return dist
            
        visitados.add((r, c))
        
        for dr, dc in [(-1, 0), (1, 0), (0, -1), (0, 1)]:
            nr, nc = r + dr, c + dc
            if 0 <= nr < linhas and 0 <= nc < colunas and grid[nr][nc] == 0:
                if (nr, nc) not in visitados:
                    fila.append((nr, nc, dist + 1))
    return -1
```

A continuación se proporciona una función en Python que debería devolver la lista de palabras únicas de una cadena en el orden en que aparecen por primera vez, pero contiene un defecto. La función debe cumplir con el siguiente requisito de comportamiento exacto:

- Para cualquier cadena de entrada `s`, devolver una lista de las palabras que aparecen en `s`, en el orden de su primera aparición, sin duplicados.
- Las palabras se definen como secuencias contiguas de caracteres que no son espacios en blanco (usar `str.split()`).
- Para una cadena vacía o una cadena que solo contiene espacios en blanco, devolver una lista vacía.
- La función no debe mutar la entrada.
- La función debe ejecutarse en tiempo O(n) donde n = len(s).

Implementación con defectos:

```python
def palabras_unicas(s: str) -> list:
    if not s:
        return []
    if s.strip() == "":
        return []
    palabras = s.split()
    vistas = set()
    resultado = []
    for p in palabras:
        if p not in vistas:
            vistas.add(p)
            resultado.append(p)
    return resultado[::-1]
```

Each rank position (1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th) in each category was held by exactly one team. Determine the full category assignment for each team and the rank each team received in its own category. Provide your answer as a JSON object with team names as keys and values containing "category" and "rank" fields. Also include a top-level "overall_scores" object mapping each team to its overall score.

Five boxes (A, B, C, D, E) are placed in a line. Box A is to the immediate left of Box B. Box C is to the immediate right of Box D. Box D is to the immediate right of Box B. Box E is at one of the ends. Box A is not at either end. Which box is in the middle of the line?

At the annual Meridian Harbor regatta, five boats—Aurora, Borealis, Celeste, Drifter, and Eclipse—competed in a single race with no ties. The following facts are known:

1. Aurora finished ahead of Borealis but behind Celeste.
2. Drifter finished exactly one position behind Borealis.
3. Eclipse finished neither first nor last.
4. The boat that finished third was not Eclipse.

Determine the exact finishing order from first to fifth. Return your answer as a JSON object with a single key "order" whose value is a list of the five boat names in order from first place to fifth place, for example: {"order": ["Celeste", "Aurora", "Borealis", "Drifter", "Eclipse"]}.

A company has four employees: Leo, Mia, Nora, and Owen. Each works in a different department: HR, Finance, IT, or Sales. Leo does not work in IT or Sales. Mia works in Finance. Nora does not work in IT. Which department does Owen work in?

Four friends—Ari, Blake, Casey, and Drew—each own exactly one of four distinct pets: a ferret, a hedgehog, an iguana, and a jackrabbit. Each pet has a different primary color: amber, blue, crimson, or jade. The following facts are all true:

1. Ari does not own the amber pet.
2. The person who owns the hedgehog owns the jade pet.
3. Casey owns the crimson pet.
4. Drew owns the iguana.
5. Blake does not own the jackrabbit.
6. The person who owns the ferret does not own the blue pet.

Determine the complete mapping of each friend to their pet and its color. Return your answer as a JSON object where each key is a friend's name and each value is an object with two keys: "pet" and "color". Use only the values provided.
A sequence of numbers starts with 3. To get the next number, you double the current number if it is odd, or divide it by 2 and add 5 if it is even. What is the fourth number in this sequence?

A scheduling puzzle. Five employees — Anna, Ben, Cara, Dan, and Eli — must each be assigned to exactly one of five shifts: Morning, Midday, Afternoon, Evening, and Night. The following constraints all hold:

1. Anna is assigned to Morning.
2. Ben is not assigned to Midday and is not assigned to Afternoon.
3. Cara is assigned to either Afternoon or Evening.
4. Dan is assigned to either Evening or Night.
5. Eli is not assigned to Night.
6. The Midday shift is assigned to either Dan or Eli.

Determine the complete assignment of each employee to a shift. Return your answer as a JSON object with one field per employee, mapping the employee name to the assigned shift. Use exactly these keys: "Anna", "Ben", "Cara", "Dan", "Eli". Use exactly these shift values: "Morning", "Midday", "Afternoon", "Evening", "Night".
Três amigas, Ana, Bia e Carla, estão em uma fila de três pessoas. Ana está atrás de Bia. Carla está imediatamente na frente de Bia. Quem está na primeira posição da fila?

Rompecabezas de programación. Cinco empleados — Ana, Bruno, Carla, Diego y Elena — deben ser asignados cada uno a exactamente uno de cinco turnos: Mañana, Mediodía, Tarde, Atardecer y Noche. Se cumplen todas las siguientes restricciones:

1. Ana está asignada a Mañana.
2. Bruno no está asignado a Mediodía ni a Tarde.
3. Carla está asignada a Tarde o a Atardecer.
4. Diego está asignado a Atardecer o a Noche.
5. Elena no está asignada a Noche.
6. El turno de Mediodía está asignado a Diego o a Elena.

Determina la asignación completa de cada empleado a un turno. Devuelve tu respuesta como un objeto JSON con un campo por empleado, mapeando el nombre del empleado al turno asignado. Usa exactamente estas claves: "Ana", "Bruno", "Carla", "Diego", "Elena". Usa exactamente estos valores de turno: "Mañana", "Mediodía", "Tarde", "Atardecer", "Noche".
In a race, Diana finished behind Chloe but ahead of Elena. Beatrice finished ahead of Chloe but behind Abigail. Who finished in fourth place out of the five runners?

A seating puzzle. Six guests — P, Q, R, S, T, and U — must be seated in six chairs arranged in a single row from position 1 (leftmost) to position 6 (rightmost). Each guest occupies exactly one position, and each position holds exactly one guest. The following constraints all hold:

1. P sits in position 3.
2. Q sits immediately to the left of R (Q's position is one less than R's position).
3. S sits in an odd-numbered position (1, 3, or 5).
4. T sits in position 6.
5. U does not sit in position 1.
6. R does not sit in position 6.

Determine the complete seating arrangement. Return your answer as a JSON object mapping each position number (as a string key "1" through "6") to the guest seated there. Use exactly these guest labels: "P", "Q", "R", "S", "T", "U".

A drawer contains 4 red socks, 6 blue socks, and 8 green socks. What is the minimum number of socks you must pull out in the dark to guarantee that you have at least one pair of matching socks of any color?

At the Meridian Gallery, five paintings are hung in a straight line, positions 1 through 5 from left to right. Each painting is by a different artist and depicts a different subject. The artists are: Fiora, Gable, Hoshi, Ingram, and Jarek. The subjects are: bridge, cathedral, orchard, river, and volcano.

The curator recorded these facts:
1. Fiora’s painting is immediately left of the orchard.
2. The cathedral is in position 5.
3. Gable painted the river.
4. Hoshi’s painting is somewhere to the right of the volcano.
5. The bridge is in position 1.
6. Jarek’s painting is immediately right of Gable’s.
7. Ingram did not paint the cathedral.

Determine the artist and subject for each position. Return your answer as a JSON object whose keys are the position numbers 1–5 and whose values are objects with two keys: "artist" and "subject".

Four cups (red, blue, green, and yellow) are placed in a row. The red cup is next to the green cup but not next to the yellow cup. The blue cup is at the far right. The green cup is to the left of the red cup. What color is the cup in the second position from the left?

At the Meridian Gallery, five paintings hang in a straight row, labeled positions 1 through 5 from left to right. The paintings are: Azure Dawn, Brass Noon, Copper Dusk, Emerald Eve, and Frost Night. Each was painted by a different artist: one by Ingrid, one by Jarek, one by Kael, one by Liora, and one by Maren. Use the following clues to determine the exact left-to-right order of the paintings and their artists.

1. Brass Noon is immediately to the left of the painting by Maren.
2. Copper Dusk is somewhere to the right of Azure Dawn but somewhere to the left of Frost Night.
3. The painting by Jarek is in position 3.
4. Emerald Eve is not next to the painting by Kael.
5. The painting by Liora is immediately to the right of the painting by Ingrid.
6. Azure Dawn is not in position 1.

Return your answer as a JSON object with two arrays: "paintings" giving the painting titles in positions 1–5, and "artists" giving the corresponding artists in positions 1–5.

A train leaves Station A at 8:00 AM, traveling towards Station B at 60 mph. Another train leaves Station B at 8:30 AM traveling towards Station A on a parallel track at 80 mph. The distance between the stations is 100 miles. At what time do the trains pass each other?

At the annual Meridian Science Fair, five students—Ada, Ben, Celia, Diego, and Eli—each present exactly one project in a distinct field: astronomy, botany, chemistry, geology, or physics. The judges make the following observations:

1. Celia's project is in astronomy if and only if Diego's project is not in geology.

3. Ada's project is in botany or Celia's project is in astronomy, but not both.

Determine the field of each student's project. Return your answer as a JSON object mapping each student's name to their field, using exactly these keys: "Ada", "Ben", "Celia", "Diego", "Eli".

Em uma prateleira, temos cinco livros de cores diferentes: azul, verde, vermelho, amarelo e preto. O livro vermelho está imediatamente à esquerda do livro azul. O livro preto está na extremidade direita. O livro verde está em algum lugar à esquerda do livro vermelho. O livro amarelo está imediatamente à direita do livro azul. Qual é a cor do livro do meio?

Cinco amigos (Ana, Bruno, Carmen, Diego y Elena) se sientan en una fila de cinco sillas numeradas del 1 al 5, de izquierda a derecha. Se cumplen todas las siguientes condiciones:

1. Ana no se sienta en la silla 1 ni en la silla 5.

Determina la asignación completa (silla y nombre para cada posición 1-5) y responde únicamente con un objeto JSON con el siguiente formato exacto:

{"asientos": [{"silla": 1, "nombre": "..."}, {"silla": 2, "nombre": "..."}, {"silla": 3, "nombre": "..."}, {"silla": 4, "nombre": "..."}, {"silla": 5, "nombre": "..."}]}

No incluyas texto adicional fuera del JSON.

Alice, Bob, and Charlie each wear a different colored hat (Red, Blue, or Green) and have a different favorite food (Pizza, Burger, or Pasta).

A delivery company uses three trucks (T1, T2, T3) to deliver packages to three cities (A, B, C). Each truck delivers to exactly one city, and each city receives exactly one truck. The following constraints all hold:

{"assignments": [{"truck": "T1", "city": "..."}, {"truck": "T2", "city": "..."}, {"truck": "T3", "city": "..."}]}

Do not include any text outside the JSON.

Five runners (A, B, C, D, and E) finished a race in different positions from first to fifth. There were no ties.
- Runner A finished immediately ahead of Runner B.

Four employees (P, Q, R, S) are scheduled to work on four different days of one week: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday. Each employee works exactly one of these days, and each day has exactly one employee. All of the following conditions hold:

4. S works on the day immediately before the day Q works on (with Monday considered the day immediately before Tuesday, and so on).

{"schedule": [{"day": "Monday", "employee": "..."}, {"day": "Tuesday", "employee": "..."}, {"day": "Wednesday", "employee": "..."}, {"day": "Thursday", "employee": "..."}]}

Do not include any text outside the JSON.

Three houses in a row are numbered 1, 2, and 3 from left to right. Each house is painted a different color (Red, Yellow, or Blue) and contains a different pet (Dog, Cat, or Goldfish).
- House 3 is Yellow.
- The Goldfish lives in the Blue house.
- The Dog lives in the house immediately to the right of the Red house.

A scheduling puzzle. Five employees — Ana, Bruno, Carla, Diego, and Elena — must each work exactly one of five shifts: Morning, Midday, Afternoon, Evening, or Night. The following constraints all hold:

1. Ana works the Morning shift.

3. Carla works a shift that comes strictly before Diego's shift in the listed order (Morning < Midday < Afternoon < Evening < Night).

In a certain land, every inhabitant is either a Knight (who always tells the truth) or a Knave (who always lies). You meet two inhabitants named X and Y.

A box contains five numbered balls: 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5. Three balls are drawn one at a time without replacement, producing a sequence of three digits in the order drawn. The following conditions all hold:

1. The first digit drawn is even.
2. The second digit drawn is greater than the first digit.
3. The third digit drawn is odd.
4. The digit 5 is drawn at some position in the sequence.
5. The digit 3 is not drawn.

List every possible three-digit sequence (as a string of three digits, e.g., "245") that satisfies all five conditions. Return your answer as a JSON object with a single key "sequences" whose value is a JSON array of all valid three-digit strings, in ascending lexicographic order with no duplicates.

Ana, Bruno e Carlos usam, cada um, um chapéu de cor diferente (Vermelho, Azul ou Verde) e preferem uma bebida diferente (Suco, Chá ou Café).

- A pessoa que usa o chapéu Vermelho prefere Suco.

Un rompecabezas de horarios. Seis empleados — Ana, Bruno, Carla, Diego, Elena y Felipe — deben trabajar cada uno exactamente uno de seis turnos: Mañana, Mediodía, Tarde, Atardecer, Noche o Madrugada. El orden de los turnos es Mañana < Mediodía < Tarde < Atardecer < Noche < Madrugada. Se cumplen todas las siguientes condiciones:

1. Ana trabaja en el turno de Mañana.
2. Felipe no trabaja en el turno de Mediodía ni en el de Tarde.
3. El turno de Diego es estrictamente posterior al turno de Carla en el orden indicado.
4. Elena trabaja en el turno de Noche.
5. El turno de Mediodía lo trabajan Carla o Diego (solo uno de ellos).
6. Bruno trabaja en un turno que es estrictamente anterior al turno de Felipe.

Determina la asignación completa de un turno a cada empleado. Devuelve tu respuesta como un objeto JSON con las claves "Ana", "Bruno", "Carla", "Diego", "Elena" y "Felipe", cada una asociada a uno de: "Mañana", "Mediodía", "Tarde", "Atardecer", "Noche" o "Madrugada".

Four meetings (M1, M2, M3, and M4) are scheduled from Monday to Thursday, with exactly one meeting per day.
- M1 must be scheduled on a day earlier than M2.
- M3 must be scheduled on either Tuesday or Wednesday.
- M4 must be scheduled on the day immediately following M1.
On which day of the week is M4 scheduled?

A library has five shelves numbered 1 through 5 from top to bottom. Each shelf holds exactly one book, and each book has a distinct color from the set {Red, Blue, Green, Yellow, White}. The following conditions all hold:

1. The Red book is on a higher-numbered shelf than the Blue book.
2. The Green book is immediately above the Yellow book (Green is directly on top of Yellow, with no shelf between them).
3. Shelf 3 does not contain the Red book and does not contain the White book.
4. The Blue book is on shelf 1 or shelf 2.
5. The White book is on shelf 5.

Determine the color of the book on each shelf (shelf 1 through shelf 5). Return your answer as a JSON object with keys "shelf_1", "shelf_2", "shelf_3", "shelf_4", and "shelf_5", where each value is one of: "Red", "Blue", "Green", "Yellow", "White".

Three boxes (A, B, and C) are on a table. One box contains a gold coin, and the other two boxes are empty. Each box has a statement written on it, but only one of the statements is true:
- Box A: 'The gold coin is in Box B.'
- Box B: 'The gold coin is not in Box B.'
- Box C: 'The gold coin is not in Box A.'

Six friends — Anna, Ben, Cara, Dan, Eve, and Finn — sit in a row of six chairs numbered 1 through 6 from left to right. Each person has a distinct favorite fruit from the set {Apple, Banana, Cherry, Date, Elderberry, Fig}. The following conditions all hold:

2. The person who likes Apples sits immediately to the right of the person who likes Bananas.
3. Ben likes Cherries.
4. The person who likes Dates sits in chair 6.
5. Cara sits immediately to the left of Dan.
6. Eve does not sit in chair 3 and does not like Elderberries.

Determine, for each chair (1 through 6), the name of the person sitting there and their favorite fruit. Return your answer as a JSON object with keys "chair_1", "chair_2", "chair_3", "chair_4", "chair_5", and "chair_6". Each value must be an object with keys "name" and "fruit", where "name" is one of "Anna", "Ben", "Cara", "Dan", "Eve", "Finn" and "fruit" is one of "Apple", "Banana", "Cherry", "Date", "Elderberry", "Fig".

Four friends (Liam, Mia, Noah, and Olivia) have different heights.
- Liam is taller than Mia but shorter than Olivia.
- Mia is not the shortest.
Who is the shortest of the four friends?

A company has four departments — HR, IT, Sales, and Operations — each led by a different manager from the set {Maya, Nick, Olivia, Paul}. Each department occupies one floor of a four-story building, with floors numbered 1 through 4 from bottom to top. The following conditions all hold:

2. The Sales department is on floor 2.
3. Maya leads the HR department.
4. Nick's department is on floor 4.
5. Olivia does not lead the Operations department.
6. The Operations department is on a lower floor than the Sales department.

Determine, for each floor (1 through 4), the department located there and the manager who leads it. Return your answer as a JSON object with keys "floor_1", "floor_2", "floor_3", and "floor_4". Each value must be an object with keys "department" and "manager", where "department" is one of "HR", "IT", "Sales", "Operations" and "manager" is one of "Maya", "Nick", "Olivia", "Paul".

A chef needs to prepare three dishes: Soup, Salad, and Dessert, one after another.
- The Soup cannot be prepared last.
- The Dessert must be prepared immediately after the Salad.
Which dish is prepared second?

A small office has five employees: Anna, Ben, Cara, Dan, and Eve. Each works on exactly one of three projects: Project X, Project Y, or Project Z. The following facts are all true:

Example shape: {"assignment": {"Anna": "X", "Ben": "X", "Cara": "Y", "Dan": "Y", "Eve": "Z"}}.

Quatro corredores (Ana, Beatriz, Caio e Daniel) terminaram uma corrida em posições diferentes do primeiro ao quarto lugar. Não houve empates.
- Ana terminou imediatamente antes de Beatriz.
- Caio não terminou em último lugar.

Una biblioteca tiene cuatro estanterías numeradas 1, 2, 3 y 4. En cada estantería se coloca exactamente uno de los cuatro libros: "Cien años de soledad", "Don Quijote", "La Odisea" y "El Quijote" (los dos últimos son ediciones distintas). Se cumplen todas las siguientes condiciones:

1. El libro "Don Quijote" está en una estantería con número menor que "La Odisea".
2. "Cien años de soledad" está en la estantería 2.

Determina la ubicación única de cada libro. Devuelve tu respuesta como un objeto JSON con una sola clave "ubicacion", cuyo valor sea un objeto que asocie cada título de libro con el número de estantería (1, 2, 3 o 4). Ejemplo de forma: {"ubicacion": {"Cien años de soledad": 2, "Don Quijote": 1, "La Odisea": 4, "El Quijote": 3}}.

Alice, Bob, and Charlie are standing in a straight line from left to right.

A delivery driver must visit four cities: A, B, C, and D, starting and ending at A. The driver must visit B, C, and D each exactly once, in some order, between the two visits to A. Find the unique route (starting and ending at A) that yields the minimum total distance. Return your answer as a JSON object with one key, "route", whose value is an array of city letters in visit order, starting and ending with A. Example shape: {"route": ["A", "B", "C", "D", "A"]}.

Three boxes (Red, Blue, and Green) are on a table. Exactly one of them contains a key, while the other two are empty. You are given the following clues:

At the annual Meridian Valley puzzle fair, five friends—Ada, Ben, Celia, Dan, and Eve—each enter exactly one of five events: Archery, Bridge, Chess, Darts, or Escape-room. No two friends enter the same event. The following clues are all true:

Devuelve un objeto JSON con la clave "asientos" cuyo valor sea un objeto que mapee cada nombre a su número de silla (1-5). Por ejemplo: {"asientos": {"Ana": 2, "Bruno": 3, "Carlos": 4, "Diana": 1, "Elena": 2}}.

Four friends—A, B, C, and D—have different heights.

A librarian must schedule four book club meetings (M1, M2, M3, M4) on four different days of one week (Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday). Each meeting is exactly one hour long. The following constraints apply:

Three switches (labeled 1, 2, and 3) control three different colored lights (Red, Green, and Blue). Each switch controls exactly one light.
1. Switch 1 does not control the Red light.
2. Switch 2 controls the Blue light.

Which light does Switch 3 control? State only the color of the light.

Six boxes labeled A, B, C, D, E, and F are stacked in a single vertical column. Exactly one box occupies each of the six positions, numbered 1 (bottom) through 6 (top). The following conditions hold:

Five friends — Anna, Ben, Cara, Dan, and Eve — each chose a different favorite fruit from the set {apple, banana, cherry, date, elderberry}. The following statements are all true:

Six boxes are lined up in a row, numbered 1 through 6 from left to right. Each box contains a different color from the set {red, blue, green, yellow, purple, orange}. The following clues are all true:

Cuatro estudiantes — Luis, Marta, Nicolás y Olga — tienen cada uno una edad distinta del conjunto {18, 19, 20, 21}. Las siguientes afirmaciones son todas verdaderas:

Five colleagues (Alice, Bob, Charlie, David, and Eva) sit in a row of five seats, numbered 1 to 5 from left to right. Each colleague wears a different color shirt: Red, Blue, Green, Yellow, and White.

Seven researchers — P1, P2, P3, P4, P5, P6, P7 — are each assigned a distinct project from {Alpha, Beta, Gamma, Delta, Epsilon, Zeta, Eta} and a distinct office number from {101, 102, 103, 104, 105, 106, 107}. All of the following statements are true:

Four managers (Fiona, George, Hannah, and Ian) schedule meetings in four different rooms (Room A, Room B, Room C, and Room D) at four different times (9:00 AM, 10:00 AM, 11:00 AM, and 12:00 PM). A scheduling puzzle has the following constraints. Five meetings (M1-M5) must be scheduled in five consecutive time slots (S1-S5, where S1 is earliest and S5 is latest). Each meeting is assigned to exactly one slot, and each slot holds exactly one meeting. The meetings and their durations (in slots) are: M1=1, M2=2, M3=1, M4=2, M5=1. A meeting of duration 2 occupies two consecutive slots. The constraints are:
- M1 must be in S1 or S2.
- M2 must end by S4 (i.e., its second slot is at most S4).
- M3 must be immediately after M2 (the slot right after M2's second slot).
- M4 cannot be in S1.
- M5 must be in S5.
- M2 and M4 cannot overlap.

Determine the complete schedule as a JSON object mapping each meeting to its starting slot number. Output only the JSON object with keys "M1","M2","M3","M4","M5" and integer values from 1 to 5.

Four suspects (Kev, Leo, Max, and Ned) are being questioned about a theft. Each suspect has a different occupation (Baker, Tailor, Carpenter, and Mason). Exactly one of them is the thief, and exactly one of them is telling the truth (the other three are lying).

- Ned: "I am the Carpenter, and Leo is the thief."

Additionally, we know the following objective facts:
1. Ned is the Mason.

Based on this information, who is the thief? State only the name.

A logic puzzle involves five people (A, B, C, D, E) and five distinct professions (Doctor, Engineer, Lawyer, Teacher, Chef). Each person has exactly one profession, and each profession is assigned to exactly one person. The following clues are given:

Ages are distinct integers 1-5 (1=youngest, 5=oldest). Determine the profession of each person. Output a JSON object with keys "A","B","C","D","E" and values being one of "Doctor","Engineer","Lawyer","Teacher","Chef".

Five software engineers (Oliver, Penelope, Quinn, Rachel, and Sam) each lead a different project (Project Alpha, Project Beta, Project Gamma, Project Delta, and Project Epsilon) using a different programming language (Python, Java, C++, Go, and Rust).

A tournament has 6 teams (T1-T6) playing a round-robin where each pair plays exactly once. Each game results in a win for one team (no draws). The following results are known:

Cinco amigos (Ana, Bruno, Carla, Daniel e Eduardo) moram em uma fileira de cinco casas, numeradas de 1 a 5 da esquerda para a direita. Cada amigo mora em uma casa de cor diferente: Azul, Amarela, Verde, Vermelha e Branca.

Four students (Victor, Wendy, Xavier, and Yara) participated in a math competition and ranked 1st, 2nd, 3rd, and 4th. Each student wore a shirt with a different number: 10, 20, 30, and 40.

Solve the following logic puzzle and return your answer as JSON with the key "answer".

Five books (titled "History", "Science", "Art", "Music", and "Drama") are placed on a shelf in positions 1 to 5 from left to right. Each book has a different cover color: Red, Blue, Green, Yellow, and Purple.

Solve the following logic puzzle and return your answer as JSON with the key "answer".

Premises:

Five airlines (Delta, United, American, JetBlue, and Southwest) operate departing flights numbered 101, 202, 303, 404, and 505. The flights depart at 1:00, 2:00, 3:00, 4:00, and 5:00.

A scheduling puzzle. The array must list every employee exactly once.

Five people (Alice, Bob, Charlie, David, and Eva) sit in a row of five chairs, numbered 1 to 5 from left to right. Each person wears a different colored hat: Red, Blue, Green, Yellow, and Purple.

A seating puzzle. Six people — P, Q, R, S, T, and U — sit in six chairs arranged in a single row, numbered 1 through 6 from left to right. Each chair holds exactly one person. The following constraints all hold:

1. P sits in an odd-numbered chair.
2. Q sits immediately to the right of P (i.e., Q's chair number is exactly one more than P's).
3. R sits in chair 1 or chair 6.

Determine the complete seating arrangement. Return your answer as a JSON object with one key, "seating", whose value is an array of six objects, each with keys "person" (one of "P", "Q", "R", "S", "T", "U") and "chair" (an integer from 1 to 6). The array must list every person exactly once.

Quatro estudantes (Felipe, Gabriel, Helena e Isabela) têm, cada um, uma matéria favorita diferente (Matemática, História, Biologia e Geografia) e praticam um esporte diferente (Natação, Futebol, Vôlei e Basquete).

Un rompecabezas de programación. Seis proyectos — A, B, C, D, E y F — deben programarse en seis franjas horarias consecutivas numeradas del 1 al 6 (de la más temprana a la más tardía). Cada franja se asigna a exactamente un proyecto. Se cumplen todas las restricciones siguientes:

1. El proyecto A se programa en una franja impar.
2. El proyecto B se programa en una franja estrictamente posterior a la de A.
3. El proyecto C se programa en la franja 1 o en la franja 6.

5. El proyecto E se programa en una franja estrictamente anterior a la de D.
6. El proyecto F se programa en una franja inmediatamente posterior a la de E (es decir, la franja de F es exactamente una más que la de E).
7. Los proyectos D y F no están en franjas adyacentes.

Determina la programación completa. Devuelve tu respuesta como un objeto JSON con una clave, "programacion", cuyo valor sea un arreglo de seis objetos, cada uno con las claves "proyecto" (uno de "A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F") y "franja" (un entero del 1 al 6). El arreglo debe listar cada proyecto exactamente una vez.

Five colleagues—Alice, Bob, Charlie, David, and Eva—work on different floors of a five-story office building (numbered 1 to 5, from bottom to top). Each colleague works on a different floor and has a different favorite beverage: Tea, Coffee, Milk, Juice, and Soda. The following rules apply:

2. Bob works on a floor below Alice.

Identify the floor Alice works on and Charlie's favorite beverage.

At the annual Meridian Conclave, six delegates—Arlo, Brin, Cai, Dara, Ewan, and Faye—sit at a round table with six evenly spaced seats numbered 1 through 6 clockwise. Each delegate wears one of six colored sashes: amber, bronze, cobalt, emerald, fuchsia, or silver. Each delegate specializes in exactly one of six fields: astronomy, botany, cryptography, diplomacy, ethnography, or forestry. No two delegates share a color, field, or seat.

The following facts are known:

Five coworkers—Fiona, George, Hannah, Ian, and Julia—occupy five adjacent offices in a row, numbered 101 to 105 from left to right. Each coworker uses a different brand of laptop: Apple, Dell, HP, Lenovo, or Asus. The following rules are known:

At the annual Meridian Conclave, six delegates—Arlo, Brin, Cai, Dara, Ewan, and Faye—sit at a round table with six evenly spaced seats numbered 1 through 6 clockwise. Five books (A, B, C, D, and E) are arranged in a row on a shelf, numbered 1 to 5 from left to right. Each book has a different colored cover (Red, Blue, Green, Yellow, or Orange) and is written by a different author (Smith, Jones, Taylor, Brown, or Wilson). The following rules apply:

At the annual Meridian Conclave, six delegates—Alaric, Briony, Caius, Dara, Eamon, and Fiora—each represent exactly one of six regions: Ashford, Brindle, Caelum, Dunmorrow, Eversong, and Frostwick.
